<content>
1: package compiler
2: 
3: import "core:fmt"
4: import "../vm"
5: 
6: 
7: // Parser state ===================================================================================
8: 
9: MAX_LOCALS :: 256
10: 
11: Local_Binding :: struct {
12: 	name: string,
13: 	slot: int,
14: }
15: 
16: Parser := struct {
17: 	tokens: []Token,
18: 	index:  int,
19: 
20: 	// First-pass locals live in stable frame slots starting at 0.
21: 	// Temporary expression/call slots are reused after each statement.
22: 	locals:      [MAX_LOCALS]Local_Binding,
23: 	local_count: int,
24: 	temp_slot:   int,
25: }{}
26: 
27: 
28: // Token cursor ===================================================================================
29: 
30: current_token :: proc() -> Token {
31: 	return Parser.tokens[Parser.index]
32: }
33: 
34: peek_token :: proc() -> Token {
35: 	return Parser.tokens[Parser.index + 1]
36: }
37: 
38: at_token :: proc(kind: TokenKind) -> bool {
39: 	return current_token().kind == kind
40: }
41: 
42: advance_token :: proc() -> Token {
43: 	token := current_token()
44: 	Parser.index += 1
45: 	return token
46: }
47: 
48: consume_token :: proc(kind: TokenKind, message: string) -> Token {
49: 	if at_token(kind) {
50: 		return advance_token()
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	token := current_token()
54: 	panic(fmt.tprintf(
55: 		"parser error at %d:%d offset %d: %s",
56: 		token.line,
57: 		token.column,
58: 		token.offset,
59: 		message,
60: 	))
61: }
62: 
63: 
64: // Slots and locals ===============================================================================
65: 
66: alloc_temp :: proc() -> int {
67: 	slot := Parser.temp_slot
68: 	Parser.temp_slot += 1
69: 	return slot
70: }
71: 
72: reset_temps :: proc() {
73: 	Parser.temp_slot = Parser.local_count
74: }
75: 
76: declare_local :: proc(name: string) -> int {
77: 	slot := Parser.local_count
78: 	Parser.locals[Parser.local_count] = Local_Binding{name = name, slot = slot}
79: 	Parser.local_count += 1
80: 	Parser.temp_slot = Parser.local_count
81: 	return slot
82: }
83: 
84: resolve_local :: proc(name: string) -> (slot: int, ok: bool) {
85: 	for local_index := Parser.local_count - 1; local_index >= 0; local_index -= 1 {
86: 		if Parser.locals[local_index].name == name {
87: 			return Parser.locals[local_index].slot, true
88: 		}
89: 	}
90: 
91: 	return 0, false
92: }
93: 
94: 
95: // Expressions ====================================================================================
96: 
97: parse_primary_into :: proc(dst: int) {
98: 	token := advance_token()
99: 
100: 	#partial switch token.kind {
101: 	case .INT:
102: 		value, _ := token.value.(i64)
103: 		const_index := const_int(value)
104: 		emit_load_const(dst, const_index)
105: 
106: 	case .STRING:
107: 		value, _ := token.value.(string)
108: 		const_index := const_string(value)
109: 		emit_load_const(dst, const_index)
110: 
111: 	case .TRUE:
112: 		emit_load_true(dst)
113: 
114: 	case .FALSE:
115: 		emit_load_false(dst)
116: 
117: 	case .NIL:
118: 		emit_load_nil(dst)
119: 
120: 	case .IDENT:
121: 		name, _ := token.value.(string)
122: 		local_slot, is_local := resolve_local(name)
123: 		if is_local {
124: 			emit_move(dst, local_slot)
125: 		} else {
126: 			binding_id := bind_global(name)
127: 			emit_get_global(dst, binding_id)
128: 		}
129: 
130: 	case:
131: 		panic(fmt.tprintf(
132: 			"parser error at %d:%d offset %d: expected expression",
133: 			token.line,
134: 			token.column,
135: 			token.offset,
136: 		))
137: 	}
138: }
139: 
140: parse_call :: proc(callee_slot, requested_results: int) {
141: 	consume_token(.LEFT_PAREN, "expected '(' to start call arguments")
142: 
143: 	// VM call layout is slot-contiguous:
144: 	//     callee, arg0, arg1, ...
145: 	// The parser emits each argument directly into the slot after the previous one.
146: 	// Commas are the required argument separators. A trailing comma before `)` is accepted.
147: 	arg_count := 0
148: 	if !at_token(.RIGHT_PAREN) {
149: 		for {
150: 			parse_expression_into(callee_slot + 1 + arg_count, 1)
151: 			arg_count += 1
152: 
153: 			if !at_token(.COMMA) {
154: 				break
155: 			}
156: 
157: 			advance_token()
158: 			if at_token(.RIGHT_PAREN) {
159: 				break
160: 			}
161: 		}
162: 	}
163: 
164: 	consume_token(.RIGHT_PAREN, "expected ')' after call arguments")
165: 	emit_call(callee_slot, arg_count, requested_results)
166: }
167: 
168: parse_expression_into :: proc(dst: int, requested_results: int = 1) {
169: 	parse_primary_into(dst)
170: 
171: 	if at_token(.LEFT_PAREN) {
172: 		parse_call(dst, requested_results)
173: 	}
174: }
175: 
176: 
177: // Statements =====================================================================================
178: 
179: parse_local_declaration :: proc() {
180: 	name_token := consume_token(.IDENT, "expected local name")
181: 	name, _ := name_token.value.(string)
182: 
183: 	consume_token(.DECL, "expected ':=' after local name")
184: 
185: 	slot := declare_local(name)
186: 	parse_expression_into(slot)
187: }
188: 
189: parse_assignment :: proc() {
190: 	name_token := consume_token(.IDENT, "expected assignment name")
191: 	name, _ := name_token.value.(string)
192: 
193: 	slot, is_local := resolve_local(name)
194: 	if !is_local {
195: 		panic(fmt.tprintf(
196: 			"parser error at %d:%d offset %d: assignment target is not a local",
197: 			name_token.line,
198: 			name_token.column,
199: 			name_token.offset,
200: 		))
201: 	}
202: 
203: 	consume_token(.ASSIGN, "expected '=' after assignment name")
204: 	parse_expression_into(slot)
205: }
206: 
207: parse_expression_statement :: proc() {
208: 	slot := alloc_temp()
209: 	parse_expression_into(slot, 0)
210: }
211: 
212: parse_statement :: proc() {
213: 	if at_token(.IDENT) && peek_token().kind == .DECL {
214: 		parse_local_declaration()
215: 		return
216: 	}
217: 
218: 	if at_token(.IDENT) && peek_token().kind == .ASSIGN {
219: 		parse_assignment()
220: 		return
221: 	}
222: 
223: 	parse_expression_statement()
224: }
225: 
226: parse_top_level_statements :: proc() {
227: 	for !at_token(.EOF) {
228: 		parse_statement()
229: 		reset_temps()
230: 	}
231: }
232: 
233: 
234: // Source compilation =============================================================================
235: 
236: compile_source :: proc(source: string) -> vm.State {
237: 	tokens := scan_source(source)
238: 	defer delete(tokens)
239: 
240: 	Parser.tokens = tokens[:]
241: 	Parser.index = 0
242: 	Parser.local_count = 0
243: 	Parser.temp_slot = 0
244: 
245: 	begin_proto("entry", 0)
246: 	parse_top_level_statements()
247: 
248: 	result_slot := alloc_temp()
249: 	emit_load_nil(result_slot)
250: 	emit_return(result_slot, 1)
251: 
252: 	end_proto()
253: 	return build_vm_state()
254: }

(End of file - total 254 lines)
</content>

<path>C:\dev\kilnvm_dev\src\compiler\scanner.odin</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package compiler
2: 
3: import "core:fmt"
4: import "core:strconv"
5: 
6: 
7: // Tokens =========================================================================================
8: 
9: TokenKind :: enum {
10: 	// Stream markers
11: 	EOF,
12: 
13: 	// Literals
14: 	IDENT,
15: 	INT,
16: 	FLOAT,
17: 	STRING,
18: 
19: 	// Literal keywords
20: 	TRUE,
21: 	FALSE,
22: 	NIL,
23: 
24: 	// Control flow
25: 	IF,
26: 	ELSE,
27: 	FOR,
28: 	FUNCTION,
29: 	RETURN,
30: 
31: 	// Binding / construction keywords
32: 	GLOBAL,
33: 	MAP,
34: 
35: 	// Binding operators
36: 	DECL,       // :=
37: 	CONST_DECL, // ::
38: 	ASSIGN,     // =
39: 
40: 	// Arithmetic operators
41: 	PLUS,
42: 	MINUS,
43: 	STAR,
44: 	SLASH,
45: 
46: 	// Comparison / logical operators
47: 	EQUAL,
48: 	NOT,
49: 	NOT_EQUAL,
50: 	LESS,
51: 	LESS_OR_EQUAL,
52: 	GREATER,
53: 	GREATER_OR_EQUAL,
54: 
55: 	// Delimiters
56: 	LEFT_PAREN,
57: 	RIGHT_PAREN,
58: 	LEFT_BRACE,
59: 	RIGHT_BRACE,
60: 	LEFT_BRACKET,
61: 	RIGHT_BRACKET,
62: 
63: 	// Separators / access
64: 	COMMA,
65: 	DOT,
66: 	COLON,
67: 	SEMICOLON,
68: }
69: 
70: TokenValue :: union {
71: 	i64,
72: 	f64,
73: 	string,
74: }
75: 
76: Token :: struct {
77: 	kind:  TokenKind,
78: 	value: TokenValue,
79: 
80: 	offset: int, // byte index where this token begins in source
81: 	line:   int, // 1-based source line where this token begins
82: 	column: int, // 1-based source column where this token begins
83: }
84: 
85: 
86: // Scanner state ==================================================================================
87: 
88: Scanner := struct {
89: 	source: string,
90: 
91: 	// Moving scanner cursor. `index` is a byte index into `source`.
92: 	index:  int,
93: 	line:   int,
94: 	column: int,
95: 
96: 	// Start position of the token currently being scanned.
97: 	token_start:  int,
98: 	token_line:   int,
99: 	token_column: int,
100: 
101: 	tokens: [dynamic]Token,
102: }{}
103: 
104: 
105: // Cursor =========================================================================================
106: 
107: advance :: proc() -> u8 {
108: 	ch := Scanner.source[Scanner.index]
109: 	Scanner.index += 1
110: 
111: 	if ch == '\n' {
112: 		Scanner.line += 1
113: 		Scanner.column = 1
114: 	} else {
115: 		Scanner.column += 1
116: 	}
117: 
118: 	return ch
119: }
120: 
121: begin_token :: proc() {
122: 	Scanner.token_start = Scanner.index
123: 	Scanner.token_line = Scanner.line
124: 	Scanner.token_column = Scanner.column
125: }
126: 
127: match_next :: proc(expected: u8) -> bool {
128: 	if Scanner.index >= len(Scanner.source) {
129: 		return false
130: 	}
131: 
132: 	if Scanner.source[Scanner.index] != expected {
133: 		return false
134: 	}
135: 
136: 	advance()
137: 	return true
138: }
139: 
140: 
141: // Emit ===========================================================================================
142: 
143: scanner_panic :: proc(message: string) {
144: 	panic(fmt.tprintf(
145: 		"scanner error at %d:%d offset %d: %s",
146: 		Scanner.token_line,
147: 		Scanner.token_column,
148: 		Scanner.token_start,
149: 		message,
150: 	))
151: }
152: 
153: emit_token :: proc(kind: TokenKind, value: TokenValue = {}) {
154: 	append(&Scanner.tokens, Token {
155: 		kind   = kind,
156: 		value  = value,
157: 		offset = Scanner.token_start,
158: 		line   = Scanner.token_line,
159: 		column = Scanner.token_column,
160: 	})
161: }
162: 
163: 
164: // Character classes =============================================================================
165: 
166: is_alpha :: proc(ch: u8) -> bool {
167: 	return (ch >= 'a' && ch <= 'z') || (ch >= 'A' && ch <= 'Z')
168: }
169: 
170: is_digit :: proc(ch: u8) -> bool {
171: 	return ch >= '0' && ch <= '9'
172: }
173: 
174: is_ident_char :: proc(ch: u8) -> bool {
175: 	return is_alpha(ch) || is_digit(ch) || ch == '_'
176: }
177: 
178: 
179: // Token scans ====================================================================================
180: 
181: scan_ident_or_keyword :: proc() {
182: 	// Identifiers and keywords share the same character rule.
183: 	for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) {
184: 		ch := Scanner.source[Scanner.index]
185: 		if !is_ident_char(ch) {
186: 			break
187: 		}
188: 		advance()
189: 	}
190: 
191: 	text := Scanner.source[Scanner.token_start:Scanner.index]
192: 
193: 	switch text {
194: 	case "true":
195: 		emit_token(.TRUE)
196: 	case "false":
197: 		emit_token(.FALSE)
198: 	case "nil":
199: 		emit_token(.NIL)
200: 	case "if":
201: 		emit_token(.IF)
202: 	case "else":
203: 		emit_token(.ELSE)
204: 	case "for":
205: 		emit_token(.FOR)
206: 	case "function":
207: 		emit_token(.FUNCTION)
208: 	case "return":
209: 		emit_token(.RETURN)
210: 	case "global":
211: 		emit_token(.GLOBAL)
212: 	case "map":
213: 		emit_token(.MAP)
214: 	case:
215: 		emit_token(.IDENT, TokenValue(text))
216: 	}
217: }
218: 
219: scan_number :: proc() {
220: 	// First pass numbers are decimal integers, hex integers, or decimal floats. No octal or exponent form yet.
221: 	if Scanner.index + 1 < len(Scanner.source) &&
222: 	   Scanner.source[Scanner.index] == '0' &&
223: 	   Scanner.source[Scanner.index + 1] == 'x' {
224: 		advance()
225: 		advance()
226: 
227: 		hex_start := Scanner.index
228: 		for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) {
229: 			ch := Scanner.source[Scanner.index]
230: 			is_hex_digit := ('0' <= ch && ch <= '9') ||
231: 			                ('a' <= ch && ch <= 'f') ||
232: 			                ('A' <= ch && ch <= 'F')
233: 			if !is_hex_digit {
234: 				break
235: 			}
236: 			advance()
237: 		}
238: 
239: 		if Scanner.index == hex_start {
240: 			scanner_panic("expected hex digits after 0x")
241: 		}
242: 
243: 		if Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) && is_ident_char(Scanner.source[Scanner.index]) {
244: 			scanner_panic("number literal cannot be followed by identifier characters")
245: 		}
246: 
247: 		text := Scanner.source[hex_start:Scanner.index]
248: 		value, ok := strconv.parse_i64_of_base(text, 16)
249: 		if !ok {
250: 			scanner_panic(fmt.tprintf("failed to parse hex literal %q", text))
251: 		}
252: 		emit_token(.INT, TokenValue(value))
253: 		return
254: 	}
255: 
256: 	is_float := false
257: 	if Scanner.source[Scanner.index] == '.' {
258: 		is_float = true
259: 		advance()
260: 	}
261: 
262: 	for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) {
263: 		ch := Scanner.source[Scanner.index]
264: 		if !is_digit(ch) {
265: 			break
266: 		}
267: 		advance()
268: 	}
269: 
270: 	// Treat `123.foo` as INT DOT IDENT, not a malformed float.
271: 	if Scanner.index + 1 < len(Scanner.source) &&
272: 	   Scanner.source[Scanner.index] == '.' &&
273: 	   is_digit(Scanner.source[Scanner.index + 1]) {
274: 		is_float = true
275: 		advance()
276: 
277: 		for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) {
278: 			ch := Scanner.source[Scanner.index]
279: 			if !is_digit(ch) {
280: 				break
281: 			}
282: 			advance()
283: 		}
284: 	}
285: 
286: 	text := Scanner.source[Scanner.token_start:Scanner.index]
287: 	if Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) && is_ident_char(Scanner.source[Scanner.index]) {
288: 		scanner_panic("number literal cannot be followed by identifier characters")
289: 	}
290: 
291: 	if is_float {
292: 		value, ok := strconv.parse_f64(text)
293: 		if !ok {
294: 			scanner_panic(fmt.tprintf("failed to parse float literal %q", text))
295: 		}
296: 		emit_token(.FLOAT, TokenValue(value))
297: 		return
298: 	}
299: 
300: 	value, ok := strconv.parse_i64(text)
301: 	if !ok {
302: 		scanner_panic(fmt.tprintf("failed to parse int literal %q", text))
303: 	}
304: 	emit_token(.INT, TokenValue(value))
305: }
306: 
307: scan_string :: proc() {
308: 	// First pass strings are plain quoted source slices. Escapes are not interpreted yet.
309: 	advance()
310: 	string_start := Scanner.index
311: 
312: 	for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) && Scanner.source[Scanner.index] != '"' {
313: 		if Scanner.source[Scanner.index] == '\n' {
314: 			scanner_panic("unterminated string")
315: 		}
316: 		advance()
317: 	}
318: 
319: 	if Scanner.index >= len(Scanner.source) {
320: 		scanner_panic("unterminated string")
321: 	}
322: 
323: 	text := Scanner.source[string_start:Scanner.index]
324: 	advance()
325: 	emit_token(.STRING, TokenValue(text))
326: }
327: 
328: skip_line_comment :: proc() {
329: 	// Comments are source trivia for the compiler path, so they emit no token.
330: 	for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) && Scanner.source[Scanner.index] != '\n' {
331: 		advance()
332: 	}
333: }
334: 
335: scan_symbol :: proc() {
336: 	ch := advance()
337: 
338: 	switch ch {
339: 	case ':':
340: 		if match_next('=') {
341: 			emit_token(.DECL)
342: 		} else if match_next(':') {
343: 			emit_token(.CONST_DECL)
344: 		} else {
345: 			emit_token(.COLON)
346: 		}
347: 
348: 	case '=':
349: 		if match_next('=') {
350: 			emit_token(.EQUAL)
351: 		} else {
352: 			emit_token(.ASSIGN)
353: 		}
354: 
355: 	case '!':
356: 		if match_next('=') {
357: 			emit_token(.NOT_EQUAL)
358: 		} else {
359: 			emit_token(.NOT)
360: 		}
361: 
362: 	case '<':
363: 		if match_next('=') {
364: 			emit_token(.LESS_OR_EQUAL)
365: 		} else {
366: 			emit_token(.LESS)
367: 		}
368: 
369: 	case '>':
370: 		if match_next('=') {
371: 			emit_token(.GREATER_OR_EQUAL)
372: 		} else {
373: 			emit_token(.GREATER)
374: 		}
375: 
376: 	case '+':
377: 		emit_token(.PLUS)
378: 	case '-':
379: 		emit_token(.MINUS)
380: 	case '*':
381: 		emit_token(.STAR)
382: 	case '/':
383: 		if match_next('/') {
384: 			skip_line_comment()
385: 		} else {
386: 			emit_token(.SLASH)
387: 		}
388: 
389: 	case '(':
390: 		emit_token(.LEFT_PAREN)
391: 	case ')':
392: 		emit_token(.RIGHT_PAREN)
393: 	case '{':
394: 		emit_token(.LEFT_BRACE)
395: 	case '}':
396: 		emit_token(.RIGHT_BRACE)
397: 	case '[':
398: 		emit_token(.LEFT_BRACKET)
399: 	case ']':
400: 		emit_token(.RIGHT_BRACKET)
401: 
402: 	case ',':
403: 		emit_token(.COMMA)
404: 	case '.':
405: 		emit_token(.DOT)
406: 	case ';':
407: 		emit_token(.SEMICOLON)
408: 
409: 	case:
410: 		scanner_panic(fmt.tprintf("unexpected character %q", rune(ch)))
411: 	}
412: }
413: 
414: 
415: // Scanner ========================================================================================
416: 
417: scan_source :: proc(source: string) -> [dynamic]Token {
418: 	// Each scan call starts from fresh source/cursor state.
419: 	Scanner.source = source
420: 	Scanner.index = 0
421: 	Scanner.line = 1
422: 	Scanner.column = 1
423: 	Scanner.token_start = 0
424: 	Scanner.token_line = 1
425: 	Scanner.token_column = 1
426: 	Scanner.tokens = make([dynamic]Token, 0, len(source) / 4)
427: 
428: 	for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) {
429: 		ch := Scanner.source[Scanner.index]
430: 
431: 		// Whitespace is non-semantic in Kiln source.
432: 		if ch == ' ' || ch == '\t' || ch == '\r' || ch == '\n' {
433: 			advance()
434: 			continue
435: 		}
436: 
437: 		begin_token()
438: 
439: 		if is_alpha(ch) || ch == '_' {
440: 			scan_ident_or_keyword()
441: 			continue
442: 		}
443: 
444: 		if is_digit(ch) {
445: 			scan_number()
446: 			continue
447: 		}
448: 
449: 		if ch == '.' && Scanner.index + 1 < len(Scanner.source) && is_digit(Scanner.source[Scanner.index + 1]) {
450: 			scan_number()
451: 			continue
452: 		}
453: 
454: 		if ch == '"' {
455: 			scan_string()
456: 			continue
457: 		}
458: 
459: 		scan_symbol()
460: 	}
461: 
462: 	begin_token()
463: 	emit_token(.EOF)
464: 
465: 	return Scanner.tokens
466: }

(End of file - total 466 lines)
</content>

1: package compiler
2: 
3: import "../vm"
4: import "../kiln"
5: 
6: 
7: 
8: // Emitter state ===============================================================================
9: 
10: Emitter := struct {
11:     entry_function:   ^vm.ProtoFunctionObject,
12:     global_env:       vm.BindingTable,
13:     name:             string,
14:     param_count:      int,
15:     bytecode:         [dynamic]u32,
16:     const_pool:       [dynamic]vm.Value,
17:     child_protos:     [dynamic]^vm.Proto,
18:     frame_slot_count: int,
19: }{}
20: 
21: // Internals ======================================================================================
22: 
23: record_slots :: proc(slots: ..int) {
24:     for slot in slots {
25:         required_slots := slot + 1
26:         if required_slots > Emitter.frame_slot_count {
27:             Emitter.frame_slot_count = required_slots
28:         }
29:     }
30: }
31: 
32: bind_global :: proc(name: string) -> vm.BindingId {
33:     for binding_index := 0; binding_index < Emitter.global_env.count; binding_index += 1 {
34:         if Emitter.global_env.names[binding_index] == name {
35:             return vm.BindingId(binding_index)
36:         }
37:     }
38: 
39:     binding_id := vm.BindingId(Emitter.global_env.count)
40:     Emitter.global_env.names[Emitter.global_env.count] = name
41:     Emitter.global_env.count += 1
42: 
43:     return binding_id
44: }
45: 
46: bind_native_global :: proc(name: string, native_proc: vm.NativeFunction) {
47:     native_function := new(vm.NativeFunctionObject)
48:     native_function.header.kind = .NATIVE_FUNCTION
49:     native_function.name = name
50:     native_function.impl = native_proc
51: 
52:     binding_id := bind_global(name)
53:     Emitter.global_env.values[int(binding_id)] = vm.Value(cast(^vm.Object)native_function)
54: }
55: 
56: bind_global_env :: proc() {
57:     bind_native_global("print", kiln.native_print)
58:     bind_native_global("type", kiln.native_type)
59:     bind_native_global("length", kiln.native_length)
60:     bind_native_global("assert", kiln.native_assert)
61:     bind_native_global("to_string", kiln.native_to_string)
62:     bind_native_global("to_number", kiln.native_to_number)
63: }
64: 
65: // VM state construction ==========================================================================
66: 
67: build_vm_state :: proc() -> vm.State {
68:     return vm.State{
69:         entry_function = Emitter.entry_function,
70:         global_env     = Emitter.global_env,
71:     }
72: }
73: 
74: 
75: // Proto construction =============================================================================
76: 
77: begin_proto :: proc(name: string, param_count: int) {
78:     if Emitter.global_env.count == 0 {
79:         bind_global_env()
80:     }
81: 
82:     Emitter.name = name
83:     Emitter.param_count = param_count
84:     Emitter.bytecode = make([dynamic]u32)
85:     Emitter.const_pool = make([dynamic]vm.Value)
86:     Emitter.child_protos = make([dynamic]^vm.Proto)
87:     Emitter.frame_slot_count = param_count
88: }
89: 
90: end_proto :: proc() {
91:     proto := new(vm.Proto)
92:     proto^ = vm.Proto{
93:         name             = Emitter.name,
94:         bytecode         = Emitter.bytecode[:],
95:         const_pool       = Emitter.const_pool[:],
96:         child_protos     = Emitter.child_protos[:],
97:         frame_slot_count = Emitter.frame_slot_count,
98:         param_count      = Emitter.param_count,
99:     }
100: 
101:     function_object := new(vm.ProtoFunctionObject)
102:     function_object^ = vm.ProtoFunctionObject{
103:         header = vm.Object{kind = .PROTO_FUNCTION},
104:         name   = Emitter.name,
105:         impl  = proto,
106:     }
107: 
108:     Emitter.entry_function = function_object
109: }
110: 
111: 
112: 
113: // Constants ======================================================================================
114: 
115: const_int :: proc(value: i64) -> int {
116:     const_index := len(Emitter.const_pool)
117:     append(&Emitter.const_pool, vm.Value(value))
118: 
119:     return const_index
120: }
121: 
122: const_float :: proc(value: f64) -> int {
123:     const_index := len(Emitter.const_pool)
124:     append(&Emitter.const_pool, vm.Value(value))
125: 
126:     return const_index
127: }
128: 
129: const_string :: proc(text: string) -> int {
130:     string_object := new(vm.StringObject)
131:     string_object.header.kind = .STRING
132:     string_object.data = text
133: 
134:     const_index := len(Emitter.const_pool)
135:     append(&Emitter.const_pool, vm.Value(cast(^vm.Object)string_object))
136: 
137:     return const_index
138: }
139: 
140: // Instruction Emitters ======================================================================================
141: 
142: // Loads ==========================================================================================
143: 
144: emit_load_nil :: proc(dst: int) {
145:     record_slots(dst)
146: 
147:     inst := u32(vm.InstAx{ op= .LOAD_NIL, a= u32(dst) })
148:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
149: }
150: 
151: emit_load_true :: proc(dst: int) {
152:     record_slots(dst)
153: 
154:     inst := u32(vm.InstAx{ op= .LOAD_TRUE, a= u32(dst) })
155:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
156: }
157: 
158: emit_load_false :: proc(dst: int) {
159:     record_slots(dst)
160: 
161:     inst := u32(vm.InstAx{ op= .LOAD_FALSE, a= u32(dst) })
162:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
163: }
164: 
165: emit_load_const :: proc(dst, const_index: int) {
166:     record_slots(dst)
167: 
168:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .LOAD_CONST, a= u8(dst), b= u16(const_index) })
169:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
170: }
171: 
172: emit_load_func :: proc(dst, child_proto_index: int) {
173:     record_slots(dst)
174: 
175:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .LOAD_FUNC, a= u8(dst), b= u16(child_proto_index) })
176:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
177: }
178: 
179: emit_move :: proc(dst, src: int) {
180:     record_slots(dst, src)
181: 
182:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .MOVE, a= u8(dst), b= u16(src) })
183:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
184: }
185: 
186: 
187: // Array operations ===============================================================================
188: 
189: emit_new_array :: proc(dst, array_cap: int) {
190:     record_slots(dst)
191: 
192:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .NEW_ARRAY, a= u8(dst), b= u16(array_cap) })
193:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
194: }
195: 
196: emit_array_len :: proc(dst, src_array: int) {
197:     record_slots(dst, src_array)
198: 
199:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .ARRAY_LEN, a= u8(dst), b= u16(src_array) })
200:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
201: }
202: 
203: emit_array_get :: proc(dst, src_array, index: int) {
204:     record_slots(dst, src_array, index)
205: 
206:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .ARRAY_GET, a= u8(dst), b= u8(src_array), c= u8(index) })
207:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
208: }
209: 
210: emit_array_set :: proc(dst_array, src, index: int) {
211:     record_slots(dst_array, src, index)
212: 
213:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .ARRAY_SET, a= u8(dst_array), b= u8(src), c= u8(index) })
214:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
215: }
216: 
217: emit_array_push :: proc(dst_array, src: int) {
218:     record_slots(dst_array, src)
219: 
220:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .ARRAY_PUSH, a= u8(dst_array), b= u16(src) })
221:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
222: }
223: 
224: emit_array_pop :: proc(dst, src_array: int) {
225:     record_slots(dst, src_array)
226: 
227:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .ARRAY_POP, a= u8(dst), b= u16(src_array) })
228:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
229: }
230: 
231: 
232: // Map operations =================================================================================
233: 
234: emit_new_map :: proc(dst: int) {
235:     record_slots(dst)
236: 
237:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .NEW_MAP, a= u8(dst), b= 0 })
238:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
239: }
240: 
241: emit_map_len :: proc(dst, src_map: int) {
242:     record_slots(dst, src_map)
243: 
244:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .MAP_LEN, a= u8(dst), b= u16(src_map) })
245:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
246: }
247: 
248: emit_map_get :: proc(dst, src_map, key: int) {
249:     record_slots(dst, src_map, key)
250: 
251:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .MAP_GET, a= u8(dst), b= u8(src_map), c= u8(key) })
252:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
253: }
254: 
255: emit_map_set :: proc(dst_map, key, src: int) {
256:     record_slots(dst_map, key, src)
257: 
258:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .MAP_SET, a= u8(dst_map), b= u8(key), c= u8(src) })
259:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
260: }
261: 
262: 
263: // Numeric operations =============================================================================
264: 
265: emit_add :: proc(dst, lhs, rhs: int) {
266:     record_slots(dst, lhs, rhs)
267: 
268:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .ADD, a= u8(dst), b= u8(lhs), c= u8(rhs) })
269:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
270: }
271: 
272: emit_sub :: proc(dst, lhs, rhs: int) {
273:     record_slots(dst, lhs, rhs)
274: 
275:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .SUB, a= u8(dst), b= u8(lhs), c= u8(rhs) })
276:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
277: }
278: 
279: emit_mul :: proc(dst, lhs, rhs: int) {
280:     record_slots(dst, lhs, rhs)
281: 
282:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .MUL, a= u8(dst), b= u8(lhs), c= u8(rhs) })
283:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
284: }
285: 
286: emit_div :: proc(dst, lhs, rhs: int) {
287:     record_slots(dst, lhs, rhs)
288: 
289:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .DIV, a= u8(dst), b= u8(lhs), c= u8(rhs) })
290:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
291: }
292: 
293: emit_neg :: proc(dst, src: int) {
294:     record_slots(dst, src)
295: 
296:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .NEG, a= u8(dst), b= u16(src) })
297:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
298: }
299: 
300: 
301: // Comparison and boolean operations ==============================================================
302: 
303: emit_equal :: proc(dst, lhs, rhs: int) {
304:     record_slots(dst, lhs, rhs)
305: 
306:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .EQUAL, a= u8(dst), b= u8(lhs), c= u8(rhs) })
307:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
308: }
309: 
310: emit_less :: proc(dst, lhs, rhs: int) {
311:     record_slots(dst, lhs, rhs)
312: 
313:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .LESS, a= u8(dst), b= u8(lhs), c= u8(rhs) })
314:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
315: }
316: 
317: emit_less_or_equal :: proc(dst, lhs, rhs: int) {
318:     record_slots(dst, lhs, rhs)
319: 
320:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .LESS_OR_EQUAL, a= u8(dst), b= u8(lhs), c= u8(rhs) })
321:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
322: }
323: 
324: emit_not :: proc(dst, src: int) {
325:     record_slots(dst, src)
326: 
327:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .NOT, a= u8(dst), b= u16(src) })
328:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
329: }
330: 
331: 
332: // Jumps and patching =============================================================================
333: 
334: // Jump offsets are relative to the instruction after the jump.
335: // Current code assumes generated offsets fit the VM instruction layouts.
336: 
337: next_inst_index :: proc() -> int {
338:     return len(Emitter.bytecode)
339: }
340: 
341: emit_jump :: proc(target_index: int = -1) -> int {
342:     jump_index := next_inst_index()
343:     offset := 0
344:     if target_index >= 0 {
345:         offset = target_index - (jump_index + 1)
346:     }
347: 
348:     inst := u32(vm.InstJump{ op= .JUMP, offset= i32(offset) })
349:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
350: 
351:     return jump_index
352: }
353: 
354: emit_jump_false :: proc(cond_slot: int, target_index: int = -1) -> int {
355:     record_slots(cond_slot)
356: 
357:     jump_index := next_inst_index()
358:     offset := 0
359:     if target_index >= 0 {
360:         offset = target_index - (jump_index + 1)
361:     }
362: 
363:     inst := u32(vm.InstAsBx{ op= .JUMP_FALSE, a= u8(cond_slot), sb= i16(offset) })
364:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
365: 
366:     return jump_index
367: }
368: 
369: patch_jump :: proc(jump_index: int) {
370:     word := Emitter.bytecode[jump_index]
371:     target_index := next_inst_index()
372:     offset := target_index - (jump_index + 1)
373: 
374:     op := vm.decode_op(word)
375:     if op == .JUMP {
376:         inst := u32(vm.InstJump{ op= .JUMP, offset= i32(offset) })
377:         Emitter.bytecode[jump_index] = inst
378:         return
379:     }
380: 
381:     if op == .JUMP_FALSE {
382:         old_inst := vm.InstAsBx(word)
383:         inst := u32(vm.InstAsBx{ op= .JUMP_FALSE, a= old_inst.a, sb= i16(offset) })
384:         Emitter.bytecode[jump_index] = inst
385:         return
386:     }
387: 
388:     panic("patch_jump expected JUMP or JUMP_FALSE")
389: }
390: 
391: 
392: // Calls and returns ==============================================================================
393: 
394: emit_call :: proc(call_base, arg_count, requested_results: int) {
395:     occupied_call_slots := arg_count + 1
396:     if requested_results > occupied_call_slots {
397:         occupied_call_slots = requested_results
398:     }
399:     record_slots(call_base + occupied_call_slots - 1)
400: 
401:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .CALL, a= u8(call_base), b= u8(arg_count), c= u8(requested_results) })
402:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
403: }
404: 
405: 
406: emit_return :: proc(first_slot, result_count: int) {
407:     if result_count > 0 {
408:         record_slots(first_slot + result_count - 1)
409:     }
410: 
411:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .RETURN, a= u8(first_slot), b= u16(result_count) })
412:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
413: }
414: 
415: emit_halt :: proc() {
416:     inst := u32(vm.InstABC{ op= .HALT })
417:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
418: }
419: 
420: 
421: // Global bindings ================================================================================
422: 
423: emit_get_global :: proc(dst: int, binding_id: vm.BindingId) {
424:     record_slots(dst)
425: 
426:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .GET_GLOBAL, a= u8(dst), b= u16(int(binding_id)) })
427:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
428: }
429: 
430: emit_set_global :: proc(src: int, binding_id: vm.BindingId) {
431:     record_slots(src)
432: 
433:     inst := u32(vm.InstABx{ op= .SET_GLOBAL, a= u8(src), b= u16(int(binding_id)) })
434:     append(&Emitter.bytecode, inst)
435: }

<content>
1: package vm
2: 
3: 
4: // Opcodes ========================================================================================
5: 
6: Opcode :: enum u8 {
7:     // Immediate, Constant, and Function Loads
8:     LOAD_NIL,      // Ax: A=dst
9:     LOAD_TRUE,     // Ax: A=dst
10:     LOAD_FALSE,    // Ax: A=dst
11:     LOAD_CONST,    // ABx: A=dst, B=const
12:     LOAD_FUNC,     // ABx: A=dst, B=child_proto
13:     MOVE,          // ABx: A=dst, B=src
14: 
15:     // Array Operations
16:     NEW_ARRAY,     // ABx: A=dst,       B=initial capacity
17:     ARRAY_LEN,     // ABx: A=dst,       B=src_array
18:     ARRAY_GET,     // ABC: A=dst,       B=src_array, C=index
19:     ARRAY_SET,     // ABC: A=dst_array, B=src, C=index
20:     ARRAY_PUSH,    // ABx: A=dst_array, B=src
21:     ARRAY_POP,     // ABx: A=dst,       B=src_array
22: 
23:     // Map Operations
24:     NEW_MAP,       // ABx: A=dst,     B=reserved
25:     MAP_LEN,       // ABx: A=dst,     B=src_map
26:     MAP_GET,       // ABC: A=dst,     B=src_map, C=key
27:     MAP_SET,       // ABC: A=dst_map, B=key, C=src
28: 
29:     // Numeric Operations
30:     ADD,           // ABC: A=dst, B=lhs, C=rhs
31:     SUB,           // ABC: A=dst, B=lhs, C=rhs
32:     MUL,           // ABC: A=dst, B=lhs, C=rhs
33:     DIV,           // ABC: A=dst, B=lhs, C=rhs
34:     NEG,           // ABx: A=dst, B=src
35: 
36:     // Comparison and Boolean Operations
37:     EQUAL,         // ABC: A=dst, B=lhs, C=rhs
38:     LESS,          // ABC: A=dst, B=lhs, C=rhs
39:     LESS_OR_EQUAL, // ABC: A=dst, B=lhs, C=rhs
40:     NOT,           // ABx: A=dst, B=src
41: 
42:     // Control Flow
43:     JUMP,          // Jump: offset (relative to post-fetch instruction_index)
44:     JUMP_FALSE,    // AsBx: A=cond, B=offset; jump if slot[A] is falsey
45:     HALT,          // ABC: no operands used; stop VM and return nil
46: 
47:     // Calls and Returns
48:     CALL,          // ABC: A=callee/result base, B=arg_count, C=requested_results
49:     RETURN,        // ABx: A=first_return, B=produced_results
50: 
51:     // Global Bindings
52:     GET_GLOBAL,    // ABx: A=dst, B=binding_id
53:     SET_GLOBAL,    // ABx: A=src, B=binding_id
54: }
55: 
56: 
57: // Instruction layout types =======================================================================
58: 
59: // The real bytecode stream stays `[]u32`.
60: // These types are used to pack and decode instruction words.
61: // The opcode decides which layout applies to a given instruction word.
62: 
63: InstABC :: bit_field u32 {
64:     op: Opcode | 8,
65:     a:  u8     | 8,
66:     b:  u8     | 8,
67:     c:  u8     | 8,
68: }
69: 
70: InstABx :: bit_field u32 {
71:     op: Opcode | 8,
72:     a:  u8     | 8,
73:     b:  u16    | 16, // wide second operand
74: }
75: 
76: InstAsBx :: bit_field u32 {
77:     op: Opcode | 8,
78:     a:  u8     | 8,
79:     sb: i16    | 16, // signed wide second operand
80: }
81: 
82: InstAx :: bit_field u32 {
83:     op: Opcode | 8,
84:     a:  u32    | 24, // wide unsigned
85: }
86: 
87: InstJump :: bit_field u32 {
88:     op:     Opcode | 8,
89:     offset: i32    | 24, // wide signed jump offset
90: }
91: 
92: 
93: // Heap objects ===================================================================================
94: 
95: // Heap-backed values start with Object.
96: // Value stores heap-backed values as ^Object and uses kind to recover the full object type.
97: 
98: ObjectKind :: enum u8 {
99:     STRING,
100:     PROTO_FUNCTION,
101:     NATIVE_FUNCTION,
102:     ARRAY,
103:     MAP,
104: }
105: 
106: Object :: struct {
107:     kind: ObjectKind,
108: }
109: 
110: 
111: // String objects =================================================================================
112: 
113: StringObject :: struct {
114:     header: Object, // must be first
115:     data:   string,
116: }
117: 
118: // Array objects ==================================================================================
119: 
120: ArrayObject :: struct {
121:     header: Object, // must be first
122:     data:  [dynamic]Value,
123: }
124: 
125: // Map objects ====================================================================================
126: 
127: MapObject :: struct {
128:     header: Object, // must be first
129:     data:  map[string]Value,
130: }
131: 
132: 
133: // Functions ======================================================================================
134: 
135: // Proto is compiled bytecode function data.
136: // It is not itself the runtime function value.
137: // `bytecode` is the fixed instruction stream for the function.
138: // `const_pool` is the fixed constant table used by that bytecode.
139: // `child_protos` is the fixed table of function bodies declared inside this proto.
140: // `frame_slot_count` is the size of the function's slot window.
141: 
142: Proto :: struct {
143:     name:        string,
144:     bytecode:    []u32,
145:     const_pool:  []Value,
146:     child_protos: []^Proto,
147:     frame_slot_count: int,
148:     param_count: int,
149: }
150: 
151: // NativeFunction is an Odin-backed function implementation.
152: // Args live in vmState.slots starting at args_base.
153: // Native functions write results directly into vmState.slots starting at return_slot_base.
154: // requested_results is the exact number of result slots the caller requests.
155: // The returned int is the number of result values the native function produced.
156: 
157: NativeFunction :: proc(
158:     vm: ^State,
159:     args_base: int,
160:     arg_count: int,
161:     return_slot_base: int,
162:     requested_results: int,
163: ) -> int
164: 
165: // ProtoFunctionObject is a runtime callable object backed by bytecode.
166: // Values point to this through ^Object when header.kind == .PROTO_FUNCTION.
167: 
168: ProtoFunctionObject :: struct {
169:     header: Object, // must be first
170:     name:   string,
171:     impl:  ^Proto,
172: }
173: 
174: // NativeFunctionObject is a runtime callable object backed by an Odin procedure.
175: // Values point to this through ^Object when header.kind == .NATIVE_FUNCTION.
176: 
177: NativeFunctionObject :: struct {
178:     header:      Object, // must be first
179:     name:        string,
180:     impl: NativeFunction,
181: }
182: 
183: 
184: // Value type =====================================================================================
185: 
186: // Nil is the zero value of the union.
187: // Immediates live inline. Heap-backed values are stored as ^Object.
188: 
189: Value :: union {
190:     bool,
191:     i64,
192:     f64,
193:     ^Object,
194: }
195: 
196: 
197: // Bindings =======================================================================================
198: 
199: MAX_BINDINGS :: 256
200: 
201: BindingId :: distinct int
202: 
203: // BindingTable is a named value namespace.
204: // BindingId values are indexes into one specific BindingTable.
205: BindingTable :: struct {
206:     names:  [MAX_BINDINGS]string,
207:     values: [MAX_BINDINGS]Value,
208:     count:  int,
209: }
210: 
211: 
212: // Call frames ====================================================================================
213: 
214: // `slot_base` is the start of this call's slot window inside vmState.slots.
215: // Logical slots for this frame are addressed relative to that slot base.
216: // `return_slot_base` and `requested_results` describe where this frame must place return values
217: // in its caller's slot window. The top-level frame has no caller; top-level RETURN ends execution.
218: // `caller_slot_count` restores the caller's occupied slot range when this frame returns.
219: 
220: CallFrame :: struct {
221:     proto:                    ^Proto,
222:     instruction_index:        int,
223:     slot_base:                int,
224: 
225:     return_slot_base:         int,
226:     requested_results:   int,
227: 
228:     caller_slot_count: int,
229: }
230: 
231: 
232: // VM state =======================================================================================
233: 
234: MAX_VM_SLOTS :: 4096
235: MAX_CALLFRAMES :: 256
236: 
237: // `entry_function` is the top-level function object run by `run_vm`.
238: // LOAD_FUNC indexes the currently executing proto's child protos.
239: // CALL dispatches callable objects by Object.kind.
240: // `slots` is fixed runtime storage for all active call-frame windows.
241: // `slot_count` is the number of slots claimed by active windows.
242: // `call_frames` is fixed runtime storage for active bytecode calls.
243: // `call_frame_count` is the number of occupied entries in call_frames.
244: // The current frame is call_frames[call_frame_count - 1].
245: // `global_env` is the active global namespace.
246: 
247: State :: struct {
248:     entry_function:   ^ProtoFunctionObject,
249:     slots:            [MAX_VM_SLOTS]Value,
250:     slot_count:       int,
251: 
252:     frame_stack:      [MAX_CALLFRAMES]CallFrame,
253:     frame_count:      int,
254:     global_env:       BindingTable,
255: }
256: 
257: 
258: // Execution ======================================================================================
259: 
260: // Decode =========================================================================================
261: 
262: decode_op :: proc(word: u32) -> Opcode {
263:    return Opcode(u8(word & 0xff))
264: }
265: 
266: // Value helpers ==================================================================================
267: 
268: value_add :: proc(lhs, rhs: Value) -> Value {
269:     left_int, is_int := lhs.(i64)
270:     if is_int {
271:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
272:         if is_int {
273:             return Value(left_int + right_int)
274:         }
275: 
276:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
277:         if is_float {
278:             return Value(f64(left_int) + right_float)
279:         }
280: 
281:         panic("ADD expected numbers")
282:     }
283: 
284:     left_float, is_float := lhs.(f64)
285:     if is_float {
286:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
287:         if is_int {
288:             return Value(left_float + f64(right_int))
289:         }
290: 
291:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
292:         if is_float {
293:             return Value(left_float + right_float)
294:         }
295: 
296:         panic("ADD expected numbers")
297:     }
298: 
299:     panic("ADD expected numbers")
300: }
301: 
302: value_sub :: proc(lhs, rhs: Value) -> Value {
303:     left_int, is_int := lhs.(i64)
304:     if is_int {
305:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
306:         if is_int {
307:             return Value(left_int - right_int)
308:         }
309: 
310:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
311:         if is_float {
312:             return Value(f64(left_int) - right_float)
313:         }
314: 
315:         panic("SUB expected numbers")
316:     }
317: 
318:     left_float, is_float := lhs.(f64)
319:     if is_float {
320:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
321:         if is_int {
322:             return Value(left_float - f64(right_int))
323:         }
324: 
325:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
326:         if is_float {
327:             return Value(left_float - right_float)
328:         }
329: 
330:         panic("SUB expected numbers")
331:     }
332: 
333:     panic("SUB expected numbers")
334: }
335: 
336: value_mul :: proc(lhs, rhs: Value) -> Value {
337:     left_int, is_int := lhs.(i64)
338:     if is_int {
339:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
340:         if is_int {
341:             return Value(left_int * right_int)
342:         }
343: 
344:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
345:         if is_float {
346:             return Value(f64(left_int) * right_float)
347:         }
348: 
349:         panic("MUL expected numbers")
350:     }
351: 
352:     left_float, is_float := lhs.(f64)
353:     if is_float {
354:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
355:         if is_int {
356:             return Value(left_float * f64(right_int))
357:         }
358: 
359:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
360:         if is_float {
361:             return Value(left_float * right_float)
362:         }
363: 
364:         panic("MUL expected numbers")
365:     }
366: 
367:     panic("MUL expected numbers")
368: }
369: 
370: value_div :: proc(lhs, rhs: Value) -> Value {
371:     left_int, is_int := lhs.(i64)
372:     if is_int {
373:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
374:         if is_int {
375:             return Value(f64(left_int) / f64(right_int))
376:         }
377: 
378:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
379:         if is_float {
380:             return Value(f64(left_int) / right_float)
381:         }
382: 
383:         panic("DIV expected numbers")
384:     }
385: 
386:     left_float, is_float := lhs.(f64)
387:     if is_float {
388:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
389:         if is_int {
390:             return Value(left_float / f64(right_int))
391:         }
392: 
393:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
394:         if is_float {
395:             return Value(left_float / right_float)
396:         }
397: 
398:         panic("DIV expected numbers")
399:     }
400: 
401:     panic("DIV expected numbers")
402: }
403: 
404: value_neg :: proc(value: Value) -> Value {
405:     int_value, is_int := value.(i64)
406:     if is_int {
407:         return Value(-int_value)
408:     }
409: 
410:     float_value, is_float := value.(f64)
411:     if is_float {
412:         return Value(-float_value)
413:     }
414: 
415:     panic("NEG expected number")
416: }
417: 
418: // Comparison/truthiness helpers ==================================================================
419: 
420: // falsey = nil or false
421: // truthy = everything else
422: value_is_falsey :: proc(value: Value) -> bool {
423:     bool_value, is_bool := value.(bool)
424:     if is_bool {
425:         return !bool_value
426:     }
427: 
428:     if value == nil {
429:         return true
430:     }
431: 
432:     return false
433: }
434: 
435: value_equal :: proc(lhs, rhs: Value) -> bool {
436:     left_int, is_int := lhs.(i64)
437:     if is_int {
438:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
439:         if is_int {
440:             return left_int == right_int
441:         }
442: 
443:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
444:         if is_float {
445:             return f64(left_int) == right_float
446:         }
447: 
448:         return false
449:     }
450: 
451:     left_float, is_float := lhs.(f64)
452:     if is_float {
453:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
454:         if is_int {
455:             return left_float == f64(right_int)
456:         }
457: 
458:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
459:         if is_float {
460:             return left_float == right_float
461:         }
462: 
463:         return false
464:     }
465: 
466:     left_bool, is_bool := lhs.(bool)
467:     if is_bool {
468:         right_bool, is_bool := rhs.(bool)
469:         if is_bool {
470:             return left_bool == right_bool
471:         }
472: 
473:         return false
474:     }
475: 
476:     left_object, is_object := lhs.(^Object)
477:     if is_object {
478:         right_object, is_object := rhs.(^Object)
479:         if is_object {
480:             return left_object == right_object
481:         }
482: 
483:         return false
484:     }
485: 
486:     if lhs == nil {
487:         return rhs == nil
488:     }
489: 
490:     panic("unreachable: lhs must match one Value variant")
491: }
492: 
493: value_less :: proc(lhs, rhs: Value) -> bool {
494:     left_int, is_int := lhs.(i64)
495:     if is_int {
496:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
497:         if is_int {
498:             return left_int < right_int
499:         }
500: 
501:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
502:         if is_float {
503:             return f64(left_int) < right_float
504:         }
505: 
506:         panic("LESS expected numbers")
507:     }
508: 
509:     left_float, is_float := lhs.(f64)
510:     if is_float {
511:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
512:         if is_int {
513:             return left_float < f64(right_int)
514:         }
515: 
516:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
517:         if is_float {
518:             return left_float < right_float
519:         }
520: 
521:         panic("LESS expected numbers")
522:     }
523: 
524:     panic("LESS expected numbers")
525: }
526: 
527: value_less_or_equal :: proc(lhs, rhs: Value) -> bool {
528:     left_int, is_int := lhs.(i64)
529:     if is_int {
530:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
531:         if is_int {
532:             return left_int <= right_int
533:         }
534: 
535:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
536:         if is_float {
537:             return f64(left_int) <= right_float
538:         }
539: 
540:         panic("LESS_OR_EQUAL expected numbers")
541:     }
542: 
543:     left_float, is_float := lhs.(f64)
544:     if is_float {
545:         right_int, is_int := rhs.(i64)
546:         if is_int {
547:             return left_float <= f64(right_int)
548:         }
549: 
550:         right_float, is_float := rhs.(f64)
551:         if is_float {
552:             return left_float <= right_float
553:         }
554: 
555:         panic("LESS_OR_EQUAL expected numbers")
556:     }
557: 
558:     panic("LESS_OR_EQUAL expected numbers")
559: }
560: 
561: // VM runner ======================================================================================
562: 
563: run_vm :: proc(vm: ^State) -> Value {
564:     entry_proto := vm.entry_function.impl
565: 
566:     // Seed the first frame at slot window base 0.
567:     vm.slot_count = entry_proto.frame_slot_count
568:     vm.frame_stack[0] = CallFrame {
569:         proto                    = entry_proto,
570:         instruction_index        = 0,
571:         slot_base                = 0,
572:         return_slot_base         = 0,
573:         requested_results   = 1,
574:         caller_slot_count = 0,
575:     }
576:     vm.frame_count = 1
577: 
578:     for {
579:         // Current frame is always the last occupied call-frame entry.
580:         frame := &vm.frame_stack[vm.frame_count - 1]
581: 
582:         // Fetch then advance instruction_index.
583:         // Jump offsets are applied relative to this post-fetch index.
584:         word := frame.proto.bytecode[frame.instruction_index]
585:         frame.instruction_index += 1
586: 
587:         // Decode/dispatch by opcode, then interpret the matching layout view.
588:         // All slot operands are frame-relative:
589:         // slot[A] == vm.slots[frame.slot_base + A]
590:         switch decode_op(word) {
591:         case .HALT:
592:             return Value{}
593: 
594:         case .LOAD_NIL:
595:             inst := InstAx(word)
596:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
597:             vm.slots[dst] = Value{}
598: 
599:         case .LOAD_TRUE:
600:             inst := InstAx(word)
601:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
602:             vm.slots[dst] = Value(bool(true))
603: 
604:         case .LOAD_FALSE:
605:             inst := InstAx(word)
606:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
607:             vm.slots[dst] = Value(bool(false))
608: 
609:         case .LOAD_CONST:
610:             inst := InstABx(word)
611:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
612:             vm.slots[dst] = frame.proto.const_pool[int(inst.b)]
613: 
614:         case .LOAD_FUNC:
615:             inst := InstABx(word)
616:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
617:             child_proto := frame.proto.child_protos[int(inst.b)]
618: 
619:             function_object := new(ProtoFunctionObject)
620:             function_object.header.kind = .PROTO_FUNCTION
621:             function_object.name = child_proto.name
622:             function_object.impl = child_proto
623: 
624:             vm.slots[dst] = Value(cast(^Object)function_object)
625: 
626:         case .MOVE:
627:             inst := InstABx(word)
628:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
629:             src := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
630:             vm.slots[dst] = vm.slots[src]
631: 
632:         // NEW_ARRAY A, B
633:         // Creates an empty array in slot A. Length starts at 0.
634:         // B reserves backing capacity for future pushes (B elements).
635:         case .NEW_ARRAY:
636:             inst := InstABx(word)
637:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
638:             array_cap := int(inst.b)
639:             array_object := new(ArrayObject)
640:             array_object.header.kind = .ARRAY
641:             array_object.data = make([dynamic]Value, 0, array_cap)
642:             vm.slots[dst] = Value(cast(^Object)array_object)
643: 
644:         case .ARRAY_LEN:
645:             inst := InstABx(word)
646:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
647:             src := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
648:             header, is_object := vm.slots[src].(^Object)
649:             if !is_object || header.kind != .ARRAY {
650:                 panic("ARRAY_LEN expected array object")
651:             }
652:             array_object := cast(^ArrayObject)header
653:             vm.slots[dst] = Value(i64(len(array_object.data)))
654: 
655:         // ARRAY_GET A, B, C
656:         // Reads array[B][index in C] into slot A.
657:         case .ARRAY_GET:
658:             inst := InstABC(word)
659:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
660:             array_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
661:             index_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.c)
662: 
663:             array_header, is_object := vm.slots[array_slot].(^Object)
664:             if !is_object || array_header.kind != .ARRAY {
665:                 panic("ARRAY_GET expected array object")
666:             }
667:             array_object := cast(^ArrayObject)array_header
668: 
669:             index_i64, is_i64 := vm.slots[index_slot].(i64)
670:             if !is_i64 {
671:                 panic("ARRAY_GET expected i64 index")
672:             }
673:             if index_i64 < 0 {
674:                 panic("ARRAY_GET index out of bounds")
675:             }
676: 
677:             index := int(index_i64)
678:             if index >= len(array_object.data) {
679:                 panic("ARRAY_GET index out of bounds")
680:             }
681:             vm.slots[dst] = array_object.data[index]
682: 
683:         // ARRAY_SET A, B, C
684:         // Writes slot B into array A at index in slot C.
685:         case .ARRAY_SET:
686:             inst := InstABC(word)
687:             array_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
688:             value_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
689:             index_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.c)
690: 
691:             value := vm.slots[value_slot]
692: 
693:             array_header, is_object := vm.slots[array_slot].(^Object)
694:             if !is_object || array_header.kind != .ARRAY {
695:                 panic("ARRAY_SET expected array object")
696:             }
697:             array_object := cast(^ArrayObject)array_header
698: 
699:             index_i64, is_i64 := vm.slots[index_slot].(i64)
700:             if !is_i64 {
701:                 panic("ARRAY_SET expected i64 index")
702:             }
703:             if index_i64 < 0 {
704:                 panic("ARRAY_SET index out of bounds")
705:             }
706: 
707:             index := int(index_i64)
708:             if index >= len(array_object.data) {
709:                 panic("ARRAY_SET index out of bounds")
710:             }
711:             array_object.data[index] = value
712: 
713:         // ARRAY_PUSH A, B
714:         // Appends slot B to array in slot A.
715:         case .ARRAY_PUSH:
716:             inst := InstABx(word)
717:             array_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
718:             value_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
719: 
720:             value := vm.slots[value_slot]
721: 
722:             array_header, is_object := vm.slots[array_slot].(^Object)
723:             if !is_object || array_header.kind != .ARRAY {
724:                 panic("ARRAY_PUSH expected array object")
725:             }
726:             array_object := cast(^ArrayObject)array_header
727: 
728:             _, append_error := append(&array_object.data, value)
729:             if append_error != nil {
730:                 panic("ARRAY_PUSH failed to append")
731:             }
732: 
733:         // ARRAY_POP A, B
734:         // Pops tail of array in slot B into slot A. Empty pop is an error.
735:         case .ARRAY_POP:
736:             inst := InstABx(word)
737:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
738:             array_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
739: 
740:             array_header, is_object := vm.slots[array_slot].(^Object)
741:             if !is_object || array_header.kind != .ARRAY {
742:                 panic("ARRAY_POP expected array object")
743:             }
744:             array_object := cast(^ArrayObject)array_header
745: 
746:             popped_value, ok := pop_safe(&array_object.data)
747:             if ok {
748:                 vm.slots[dst] = popped_value
749:             } else {
750:                 panic("ARRAY_POP on empty array")
751:             }
752: 
753:         case .NEW_MAP:
754:             inst := InstABx(word)
755:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
756:             map_object := new(MapObject)
757:             map_object.header.kind = .MAP
758:             map_object.data = make(map[string]Value)
759:             vm.slots[dst] = Value(cast(^Object)map_object)
760: 
761:         case .MAP_LEN:
762:             inst := InstABx(word)
763:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
764:             map_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
765:             map_header, is_object := vm.slots[map_slot].(^Object)
766:             if !is_object || map_header.kind != .MAP {
767:                 panic("MAP_LEN expected map object")
768:             }
769:             map_object := cast(^MapObject)map_header
770:             vm.slots[dst] = Value(i64(len(map_object.data)))
771: 
772:         case .MAP_GET:
773:             inst := InstABC(word)
774:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
775:             map_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
776:             key_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.c)
777: 
778:             map_header, is_map_object := vm.slots[map_slot].(^Object)
779:             if !is_map_object || map_header.kind != .MAP {
780:                 panic("MAP_GET expected map object")
781:             }
782:             map_object := cast(^MapObject)map_header
783: 
784:             key_header, is_key_object := vm.slots[key_slot].(^Object)
785:             if !is_key_object || key_header.kind != .STRING {
786:                 panic("MAP_GET expected string key")
787:             }
788:             key_object := cast(^StringObject)key_header
789: 
790:             value, exists := map_object.data[key_object.data]
791:             if exists {
792:                 vm.slots[dst] = value
793:             } else {
794:                 vm.slots[dst] = Value{}
795:             }
796: 
797:         case .MAP_SET:
798:             inst := InstABC(word)
799:             map_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
800:             key_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
801:             value_slot := frame.slot_base + int(inst.c)
802: 
803:             map_header, is_map_object := vm.slots[map_slot].(^Object)
804:             if !is_map_object || map_header.kind != .MAP {
805:                 panic("MAP_SET expected map object")
806:             }
807:             map_object := cast(^MapObject)map_header
808: 
809:             key_header, is_key_object := vm.slots[key_slot].(^Object)
810:             if !is_key_object || key_header.kind != .STRING {
811:                 panic("MAP_SET expected string key")
812:             }
813:             key_object := cast(^StringObject)key_header
814: 
815:             value := vm.slots[value_slot]
816:             if value == nil {
817:                 delete_key(&map_object.data, key_object.data)
818:             } else {
819:                 map_object.data[key_object.data] = value
820:             }
821: 
822:         case .ADD:
823:             inst := InstABC(word)
824:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
825:             lhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
826:             rhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.c)
827:             vm.slots[dst] = value_add(vm.slots[lhs], vm.slots[rhs])
828: 
829:         case .SUB:
830:             inst := InstABC(word)
831:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
832:             lhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
833:             rhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.c)
834:             vm.slots[dst] = value_sub(vm.slots[lhs], vm.slots[rhs])
835: 
836:         case .MUL:
837:             inst := InstABC(word)
838:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
839:             lhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
840:             rhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.c)
841:             vm.slots[dst] = value_mul(vm.slots[lhs], vm.slots[rhs])
842: 
843:         case .DIV:
844:             inst := InstABC(word)
845:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
846:             lhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
847:             rhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.c)
848:             vm.slots[dst] = value_div(vm.slots[lhs], vm.slots[rhs])
849: 
850:         case .NEG:
851:             inst := InstABx(word)
852:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
853:             src := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
854:             vm.slots[dst] = value_neg(vm.slots[src])
855: 
856:         case .EQUAL:
857:             inst := InstABC(word)
858:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
859:             lhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
860:             rhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.c)
861:             vm.slots[dst] = Value(value_equal(vm.slots[lhs], vm.slots[rhs]))
862: 
863:         case .LESS:
864:             inst := InstABC(word)
865:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
866:             lhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
867:             rhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.c)
868:             vm.slots[dst] = Value(value_less(vm.slots[lhs], vm.slots[rhs]))
869: 
870:         case .LESS_OR_EQUAL:
871:             inst := InstABC(word)
872:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
873:             lhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
874:             rhs := frame.slot_base + int(inst.c)
875:             vm.slots[dst] = Value(value_less_or_equal(vm.slots[lhs], vm.slots[rhs]))
876: 
877:         case .NOT:
878:             inst := InstABx(word)
879:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
880:             src := frame.slot_base + int(inst.b)
881:             vm.slots[dst] = Value(value_is_falsey(vm.slots[src]))
882: 
883:         case .JUMP:
884:             inst := InstJump(word)
885:             frame.instruction_index += int(inst.offset)
886: 
887:         case .JUMP_FALSE:
888:             inst := InstAsBx(word)
889:             condition := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
890:             if value_is_falsey(vm.slots[condition]) {
891:                 frame.instruction_index += int(inst.sb)
892:             }
893: 
894:         case .CALL:
895:             inst := InstABC(word)
896: 
897:             // CALL A, B, C
898:             // A = callee/result base slot
899:             // B = argument count
900:             // C = requested result count
901:             call_base := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
902:             args_base := call_base + 1
903:             arg_count := int(inst.b)
904:             requested_results := int(inst.c)
905: 
906:             callee_header, is_object := vm.slots[call_base].(^Object)
907:             if !is_object {
908:                 panic("CALL expected function object")
909:             }
910: 
911:             switch callee_header.kind {
912:             case .NATIVE_FUNCTION:
913:                 // Native calls execute immediately in the caller frame.
914:                 // Native writes results at return_slot_base and reports produced count.
915:                 native_function := cast(^NativeFunctionObject)callee_header
916:                 produced_results := native_function.impl(vm, args_base, arg_count, call_base, requested_results)
917: 
918:                 if produced_results < requested_results {
919:                     // Native produced fewer than caller wants.
920:                     // Fill the missing result slots with nil.
921:                     for fill_index := produced_results; fill_index < requested_results; fill_index += 1 {
922:                         vm.slots[call_base + fill_index] = Value{}
923:                     }
924:                 }
925:                 // Extra produced results beyond requested count are ignored by contract.
926: 
927:             case .PROTO_FUNCTION:
928:                 // Proto calls push a new frame and continue the VM loop.
929:                 proto_function := cast(^ProtoFunctionObject)callee_header
930:                 callee_proto := proto_function.impl
931: 
932:                 if vm.frame_count >= MAX_CALLFRAMES {
933:                     panic("CALL exceeded VM_MAX_CALL_FRAMES")
934:                 }
935: 
936:                 callee_slot_base := args_base
937:                 callee_slot_top := callee_slot_base + callee_proto.frame_slot_count
938:                 if callee_slot_top > MAX_VM_SLOTS {
939:                     panic("CALL exceeded VM_MAX_SLOTS")
940:                 }
941: 
942:                 caller_slot_count := vm.slot_count
943:                 if callee_slot_top > vm.slot_count {
944:                     vm.slot_count = callee_slot_top
945:                 }
946: 
947:                 vm.frame_stack[vm.frame_count] = CallFrame{
948:                     proto                    = callee_proto,
949:                     instruction_index        = 0,
950:                     slot_base                = callee_slot_base,
951:                     return_slot_base         = call_base,
952:                     requested_results   = requested_results,
953:                     caller_slot_count        = caller_slot_count,
954:                 }
955:                 vm.frame_count += 1
956: 
957:             case .STRING:
958:                 panic("CALL expected function object")
959:             case .ARRAY:
960:                 panic("CALL expected function object")
961:             case .MAP:
962:                 panic("CALL expected function object")
963:             }
964: 
965:         case .GET_GLOBAL:
966:             inst := InstABx(word)
967:             dst := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
968:             binding_id := int(inst.b)
969:             vm.slots[dst] = vm.global_env.values[binding_id]
970: 
971:         case .SET_GLOBAL:
972:             inst := InstABx(word)
973:             src := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
974:             binding_id := int(inst.b)
975:             vm.global_env.values[binding_id] = vm.slots[src]
976: 
977:         case .RETURN:
978:             inst := InstABx(word)
979:             produced_slot_base := frame.slot_base + int(inst.a)
980:             produced_results := int(inst.b)
981: 
982:             if vm.frame_count == 1 {
983:                 // Top-level RETURN ends execution and returns to the host.
984:                 // Return first produced value when present, else nil.
985:                 if produced_results > 0 {
986:                     return vm.slots[produced_slot_base]
987:                 }
988:                 return Value{}
989:             }
990: 
991:             caller_result_base := frame.return_slot_base
992:             requested_results := frame.requested_results
993: 
994:             copied_result_count := produced_results
995:             if copied_result_count > requested_results {
996:                 copied_result_count = requested_results
997:             }
998: 
999:             // Copy produced values into caller result slots.
1000:             // Source and destination can overlap in the shared slot array.
1001:             if copied_result_count > 0 {
1002:                 if caller_result_base < produced_slot_base {
1003:                     for value_index := 0; value_index < copied_result_count; value_index += 1 {
1004:                         vm.slots[caller_result_base + value_index] = vm.slots[produced_slot_base + value_index]
1005:                     }
1006:                 } else {
1007:                     for value_index := copied_result_count - 1; value_index >= 0; value_index -= 1 {
1008:                         vm.slots[caller_result_base + value_index] = vm.slots[produced_slot_base + value_index]
1009:                     }
1010:                 }
1011:             }
1012: 
1013:             // Fill missing requested results with nil.
1014:             if copied_result_count < requested_results {
1015:                 for fill_index := copied_result_count; fill_index < requested_results; fill_index += 1 {
1016:                     vm.slots[caller_result_base + fill_index] = Value{}
1017:                 }
1018:             }
1019: 
1020:             // Pop current frame and restore caller's used slot range.
1021:             vm.slot_count = frame.caller_slot_count
1022:             vm.frame_count -= 1
1023:         }
1024:     }
1025: }
