<content>
1: package kiln
2: 
3: import "core:fmt"
4: import "core:strconv"
5: 
6: 
7: // Token model ====================================================================================
8: 
9: // TokenKind is the set of token types emitted by the scanner.
10: // The parser reads this token stream directly.
11: TokenKind :: enum {
12:     // Stream markers
13:     EOF,
14: 
15:     // Literals
16:     IDENT,
17:     INT,
18:     FLOAT,
19:     STRING,
20: 
21:     // Literal keywords
22:     TRUE,
23:     FALSE,
24:     NIL,
25: 
26:     // Control flow
27:     IF,
28:     ELSE,
29:     FOR,
30:     BREAK,
31:     FUNCTION,
32:     RETURN,
33: 
34:     // Binding / construction keywords
35:     GLOBAL,
36:     MAP,
37: 
38:     // Binding operators
39:     DECL,       // :=
40:     CONST_DECL, // ::
41:     ASSIGN,     // =
42: 
43:     // Arithmetic operators
44:     PLUS,
45:     MINUS,
46:     STAR,
47:     SLASH,
48: 
49:     // Comparison / logical operators
50:     EQUAL,
51:     NOT,
52:     NOT_EQUAL,
53:     LESS,
54:     LESS_OR_EQUAL,
55:     GREATER,
56:     GREATER_OR_EQUAL,
57: 
58:     // Delimiters
59:     LEFT_PAREN,
60:     RIGHT_PAREN,
61:     LEFT_BRACE,
62:     RIGHT_BRACE,
63:     LEFT_BRACKET,
64:     RIGHT_BRACKET,
65: 
66:     // Separators / access
67:     COMMA,
68:     DOT,
69:     COLON,
70:     SEMICOLON,
71: }
72: 
73: // TokenValue stores payload data for IDENT/INT/FLOAT/STRING tokens.
74: // Other token kinds leave Token.value empty.
75: TokenValue :: union {
76:     i64,
77:     f64,
78:     string,
79: }
80: 
81: // Token is one scanned unit plus its source location.
82: // offset is a byte index in source text.
83: // line and column start at 1 to match printed error locations.
84: Token :: struct {
85:     kind:  TokenKind,
86:     value: TokenValue,
87: 
88:     offset: int, // byte index where this token begins in source
89:     line:   int, // 1-based source line where this token begins
90:     column: int, // 1-based source column where this token begins
91: }
92: 
93: // Scanner state ==================================================================================
94: 
95: // Scanner is the working state used while scanning one source string.
96: // It stores source text, position, and emitted tokens.
97: // Current design runs one active scan at a time.
98: Scanner := struct {
99:     source: string,
100:     source_name: string,
101: 
102:     index:  int,
103:     line:   int,
104:     column: int,
105: 
106:     token_start:  int,
107:     token_line:   int,
108:     token_column: int,
109: 
110:     tokens: [dynamic]Token,
111:     failed: bool,
112: }{}
113: 
114: // Cursor helpers =================================================================================
115: 
116: // advance_char consumes one source byte and updates line/column.
117: // Newline increments line and resets column to 1.
118: advance_char :: proc() -> u8 {
119:     ch := Scanner.source[Scanner.index]
120:     Scanner.index += 1
121: 
122:     if ch == '\n' {
123:         Scanner.line += 1
124:         Scanner.column = 1
125:     } else {
126:         Scanner.column += 1
127:     }
128: 
129:     return ch
130: }
131: 
132: // begin_token snapshots the source position where the next token starts.
133: begin_token :: proc() {
134:     Scanner.token_start = Scanner.index
135:     Scanner.token_line = Scanner.line
136:     Scanner.token_column = Scanner.column
137: }
138: 
139: // match_next conditionally consumes one exact following byte.
140: match_next :: proc(expected: u8) -> bool {
141:     if Scanner.index >= len(Scanner.source) {
142:         return false
143:     }
144: 
145:     if Scanner.source[Scanner.index] != expected {
146:         return false
147:     }
148: 
149:     advance_char()
150:     return true
151: }
152: 
153: 
154: // Scanner errors =================================================================================
155: 
156: // scanner_error records a compile error at the current token start location.
157: // Scanner.failed stops scanning after the current step.
158: scanner_error :: proc(message: string) {
159:     location := SourceLocation{
160:         source_name = Scanner.source_name,
161:         line        = Scanner.token_line,
162:         column      = Scanner.token_column,
163:     }
164:     set_error(location, message)
165:     Scanner.failed = true
166: }
167: 
168: 
169: // Token emission =================================================================================
170: 
171: // emit_token appends one token using the current token-start snapshot.
172: emit_token :: proc(kind: TokenKind, value: TokenValue = {}) {
173:     append(&Scanner.tokens, Token {
174:         kind   = kind,
175:         value  = value,
176:         offset = Scanner.token_start,
177:         line   = Scanner.token_line,
178:         column = Scanner.token_column,
179:     })
180: }
181: 
182: 
183: // Character classes ==============================================================================
184: 
185: // Character classifiers for first-pass ASCII identifier/number rules.
186: is_alpha :: proc(ch: u8) -> bool {
187:     return (ch >= 'a' && ch <= 'z') || (ch >= 'A' && ch <= 'Z')
188: }
189: 
190: is_digit :: proc(ch: u8) -> bool {
191:     return ch >= '0' && ch <= '9'
192: }
193: 
194: is_ident_char :: proc(ch: u8) -> bool {
195:     return is_alpha(ch) || is_digit(ch) || ch == '_'
196: }
197: 
198: 
199: // Token scans ====================================================================================
200: 
201: // scan_ident_or_keyword consumes [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]* and maps keywords.
202: scan_ident_or_keyword :: proc() {
203:     for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) {
204:         ch := Scanner.source[Scanner.index]
205:         if !is_ident_char(ch) {
206:             break
207:         }
208:         advance_char()
209:     }
210: 
211:     text := Scanner.source[Scanner.token_start:Scanner.index]
212: 
213:     switch text {
214:     case "true":
215:         emit_token(.TRUE)
216:     case "false":
217:         emit_token(.FALSE)
218:     case "nil":
219:         emit_token(.NIL)
220:     case "if":
221:         emit_token(.IF)
222:     case "else":
223:         emit_token(.ELSE)
224:     case "for":
225:         emit_token(.FOR)
226:     case "break":
227:         emit_token(.BREAK)
228:     case "function":
229:         emit_token(.FUNCTION)
230:     case "return":
231:         emit_token(.RETURN)
232:     case "global":
233:         emit_token(.GLOBAL)
234:     case "map":
235:         emit_token(.MAP)
236:     case:
237:         emit_token(.IDENT, TokenValue(text))
238:     }
239: }
240: 
241: // scan_number supports:
242: // - decimal ints
243: // - hex ints with 0x prefix
244: // - decimal floats with optional leading dot (.5)
245: // Rejected here: exponent forms (1e3), octal, and identifier-suffixed numerics.
246: scan_number :: proc() {
247:     if Scanner.index + 1 < len(Scanner.source) &&
248:        Scanner.source[Scanner.index] == '0' &&
249:        Scanner.source[Scanner.index + 1] == 'x' {
250:         advance_char()
251:         advance_char()
252: 
253:         hex_start := Scanner.index
254:         for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) {
255:             ch := Scanner.source[Scanner.index]
256:             is_hex_digit := ('0' <= ch && ch <= '9') ||
257:                             ('a' <= ch && ch <= 'f') ||
258:                             ('A' <= ch && ch <= 'F')
259:             if !is_hex_digit {
260:                 break
261:             }
262:             advance_char()
263:         }
264: 
265:         if Scanner.index == hex_start {
266:             scanner_error("expected hex digits after 0x")
267:             return
268:         }
269: 
270:         if Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) && is_ident_char(Scanner.source[Scanner.index]) {
271:             scanner_error("number literal must be separated from following identifier")
272:             return
273:         }
274: 
275:         text := Scanner.source[hex_start:Scanner.index]
276:         value, ok := strconv.parse_i64_of_base(text, 16)
277:         if !ok {
278:             scanner_error(fmt.tprintf("failed to parse hex literal %q", text))
279:             return
280:         }
281:         emit_token(.INT, TokenValue(value))
282:         return
283:     }
284: 
285:     is_float := false
286:     if Scanner.source[Scanner.index] == '.' {
287:         is_float = true
288:         advance_char()
289:     }
290: 
291:     for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) {
292:         ch := Scanner.source[Scanner.index]
293:         if !is_digit(ch) {
294:             break
295:         }
296:         advance_char()
297:     }
298: 
299:     // Keep 123.foo tokenized as INT DOT IDENT.
300:     if Scanner.index + 1 < len(Scanner.source) &&
301:        Scanner.source[Scanner.index] == '.' &&
302:        is_digit(Scanner.source[Scanner.index + 1]) {
303:         is_float = true
304:         advance_char()
305: 
306:         for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) {
307:             ch := Scanner.source[Scanner.index]
308:             if !is_digit(ch) {
309:                 break
310:             }
311:             advance_char()
312:         }
313:     }
314: 
315:     text := Scanner.source[Scanner.token_start:Scanner.index]
316:     if Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) && is_ident_char(Scanner.source[Scanner.index]) {
317:         scanner_error("number literal must be separated from following identifier")
318:         return
319:     }
320: 
321:     if is_float {
322:         value, ok := strconv.parse_f64(text)
323:         if !ok {
324:             scanner_error(fmt.tprintf("failed to parse float literal %q", text))
325:             return
326:         }
327:         emit_token(.FLOAT, TokenValue(value))
328:         return
329:     }
330: 
331:     value, ok := strconv.parse_i64(text)
332:     if !ok {
333:         scanner_error(fmt.tprintf("failed to parse int literal %q", text))
334:         return
335:     }
336:     emit_token(.INT, TokenValue(value))
337: }
338: 
339: // scan_string consumes a single-line quoted string with no escape decoding.
340: scan_string :: proc() {
341:     advance_char()
342:     string_start := Scanner.index
343: 
344:     for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) && Scanner.source[Scanner.index] != '"' {
345:         if Scanner.source[Scanner.index] == '\n' {
346:             scanner_error("unterminated string")
347:             return
348:         }
349:         advance_char()
350:     }
351: 
352:     if Scanner.index >= len(Scanner.source) {
353:         scanner_error("unterminated string")
354:         return
355:     }
356: 
357:     text := Scanner.source[string_start:Scanner.index]
358:     advance_char()
359:     emit_token(.STRING, TokenValue(text))
360: }
361: 
362: // skip_line_comment consumes //... until newline and emits no token.
363: skip_line_comment :: proc() {
364:     for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) && Scanner.source[Scanner.index] != '\n' {
365:         advance_char()
366:     }
367: }
368: 
369: // scan_symbol emits punctuation/operators and handles two-character forms.
370: scan_symbol :: proc() {
371:     ch := advance_char()
372: 
373:     switch ch {
374:     case ':':
375:         if match_next('=') {
376:             emit_token(.DECL)
377:         } else if match_next(':') {
378:             emit_token(.CONST_DECL)
379:         } else {
380:             emit_token(.COLON)
381:         }
382: 
383:     case '=':
384:         if match_next('=') {
385:             emit_token(.EQUAL)
386:         } else {
387:             emit_token(.ASSIGN)
388:         }
389: 
390:     case '!':
391:         if match_next('=') {
392:             emit_token(.NOT_EQUAL)
393:         } else {
394:             emit_token(.NOT)
395:         }
396: 
397:     case '<':
398:         if match_next('=') {
399:             emit_token(.LESS_OR_EQUAL)
400:         } else {
401:             emit_token(.LESS)
402:         }
403: 
404:     case '>':
405:         if match_next('=') {
406:             emit_token(.GREATER_OR_EQUAL)
407:         } else {
408:             emit_token(.GREATER)
409:         }
410: 
411:     case '+':
412:         emit_token(.PLUS)
413:     case '-':
414:         emit_token(.MINUS)
415:     case '*':
416:         emit_token(.STAR)
417:     case '/':
418:         if match_next('/') {
419:             skip_line_comment()
420:         } else {
421:             emit_token(.SLASH)
422:         }
423: 
424:     case '(':
425:         emit_token(.LEFT_PAREN)
426:     case ')':
427:         emit_token(.RIGHT_PAREN)
428:     case '{':
429:         emit_token(.LEFT_BRACE)
430:     case '}':
431:         emit_token(.RIGHT_BRACE)
432:     case '[':
433:         emit_token(.LEFT_BRACKET)
434:     case ']':
435:         emit_token(.RIGHT_BRACKET)
436: 
437:     case ',':
438:         emit_token(.COMMA)
439:     case '.':
440:         emit_token(.DOT)
441:     case ';':
442:         emit_token(.SEMICOLON)
443: 
444:     case:
445:         scanner_error(fmt.tprintf("unexpected character %q", rune(ch)))
446:     }
447: }
448: 
449: 
450: // Source scanning ================================================================================
451: 
452: // scan_source resets Scanner state, emits a full token stream, then EOF.
453: // Caller owns the returned [dynamic]Token and must delete it.
454: scan_source :: proc(source, source_name: string) -> (tokens: [dynamic]Token, error: ^Error) {
455:     Scanner.source = source
456:     Scanner.source_name = source_name
457:     Scanner.index = 0
458:     Scanner.line = 1
459:     Scanner.column = 1
460:     Scanner.token_start = 0
461:     Scanner.token_line = 1
462:     Scanner.token_column = 1
463:     Scanner.tokens = make([dynamic]Token, 0, len(source) / 4)
464:     Scanner.failed = false
465: 
466:     for Scanner.index < len(Scanner.source) && !Scanner.failed {
467:         ch := Scanner.source[Scanner.index]
468: 
469:         // Whitespace does not emit tokens.
470:         if ch == ' ' || ch == '\t' || ch == '\r' || ch == '\n' {
471:             advance_char()
472:             continue
473:         }
474: 
475:         begin_token()
476: 
477:         if is_alpha(ch) || ch == '_' {
478:             scan_ident_or_keyword()
479:             continue
480:         }
481: 
482:         if is_digit(ch) {
483:             scan_number()
484:             continue
485:         }
486: 
487:         if ch == '.' && Scanner.index + 1 < len(Scanner.source) && is_digit(Scanner.source[Scanner.index + 1]) {
488:             scan_number()
489:             continue
490:         }
491: 
492:         if ch == '"' {
493:             scan_string()
494:             continue
495:         }
496: 
497:         scan_symbol()
498:     }
499: 
500:     if Scanner.failed {
501:         return Scanner.tokens, &Active_State.error
502:     }
503: 
504:     begin_token()
505:     emit_token(.EOF)
506: 
507:     return Scanner.tokens, nil
508: }

(End of file - total 508 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: package kiln
2: 
3: import "core:fmt"
4: 
5: // Parser state ===================================================================================
6: // working state for token-stream parsing.
7: // It tracks parse position and parse failure state for one compile operation.
8: 
9: Parser := struct {
10:     tokens: []Token,
11:     token_index: int,
12:     failed: bool,
13: }{}
14: 
15: // Token cursor ===================================================================================
16: // helpers over Parser.tokens.
17: // Parser.token_index points to the current token being parsed.
18: 
19: current_token :: proc() -> Token {
20:     return Parser.tokens[Parser.token_index]
21: }
22: 
23: peek_token :: proc() -> Token {
24:     return Parser.tokens[Parser.token_index + 1]
25: }
26: 
27: at_token :: proc(kind: TokenKind) -> bool {
28:     return current_token().kind == kind
29: }
30: 
31: advance_token :: proc() -> Token {
32: 	token := current_token()
33: 	Parser.token_index += 1
34: 	return token
35: }
36: 
37: 
38: // Parser errors ==================================================================================
39: 
40: // token_text_for_error returns source-shaped text for parser error messages.
41: token_text_for_error :: proc(token: Token) -> string {
42: 	#partial switch token.kind {
43: 	case .EOF:
44: 		return "end of file"
45: 
46: 	case .IDENT:
47: 		return token.value.(string)
48: 	case .INT:
49: 		return fmt.tprint(token.value.(i64))
50: 	case .FLOAT:
51: 		return fmt.tprint(token.value.(f64))
52: 	case .STRING:
53: 		return fmt.tprintf("\"%s\"", token.value.(string))
54: 
55: 	case .TRUE:
56: 		return "true"
57: 	case .FALSE:
58: 		return "false"
59: 	case .NIL:
60: 		return "nil"
61: 
62: 	case .IF:
63: 		return "if"
64: 	case .ELSE:
65: 		return "else"
66: 	case .FOR:
67: 		return "for"
68: 	case .BREAK:
69: 		return "break"
70: 	case .FUNCTION:
71: 		return "function"
72: 	case .RETURN:
73: 		return "return"
74: 
75: 	case .GLOBAL:
76: 		return "global"
77: 	case .MAP:
78: 		return "map"
79: 
80: 	case .DECL:
81: 		return ":="
82: 	case .CONST_DECL:
83: 		return "::"
84: 	case .ASSIGN:
85: 		return "="
86: 
87: 	case .PLUS:
88: 		return "+"
89: 	case .MINUS:
90: 		return "-"
91: 	case .STAR:
92: 		return "*"
93: 	case .SLASH:
94: 		return "/"
95: 
96: 	case .EQUAL:
97: 		return "=="
98: 	case .NOT:
99: 		return "!"
100: 	case .NOT_EQUAL:
101: 		return "!="
102: 	case .LESS:
103: 		return "<"
104: 	case .LESS_OR_EQUAL:
105: 		return "<="
106: 	case .GREATER:
107: 		return ">"
108: 	case .GREATER_OR_EQUAL:
109: 		return ">="
110: 
111: 	case .LEFT_PAREN:
112: 		return "("
113: 	case .RIGHT_PAREN:
114: 		return ")"
115: 	case .LEFT_BRACE:
116: 		return "{"
117: 	case .RIGHT_BRACE:
118: 		return "}"
119: 	case .LEFT_BRACKET:
120: 		return "["
121: 	case .RIGHT_BRACKET:
122: 		return "]"
123: 
124: 	case .COMMA:
125: 		return ","
126: 	case .DOT:
127: 		return "."
128: 	case .COLON:
129: 		return ":"
130: 	case .SEMICOLON:
131: 		return ";"
132: 	}
133: 
134: 	return fmt.tprint(token.kind)
135: }
136: 
137: // parser_error records a source compile error at a token location.
138: // This covers grammar errors and codegen limits found while the parser drives bytecode generation.
139: parser_error :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState, token: Token, message: string) {
140:     set_error(SourceLocation{
141:         source_name = proto_state.origin.source_name,
142:         line        = token.line,
143:         column      = token.column,
144:     }, message)
145:     Parser.failed = true
146: }
147: 
148: // consume_token enforces required grammar tokens.
149: // On mismatch it records an error and returns zero token value.
150: consume_token :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState, kind: TokenKind, message: string) -> Token {
151:     if at_token(kind) {
152:         return advance_token()
153:     }
154: 
155:     token := current_token()
156:     parser_error(proto_state, token, message)
157:     return Token{}
158: }
159: 
160: 
161: // Slots and locals ===============================================================================
162: 
163: // claim_temp_slot reserves one temporary frame slot for expression/call work.
164: // Temp slots are bounded by MAX_FRAME_SLOTS due to u8 slot encoding.
165: claim_temp_slot :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState) -> int {
166:     if proto_state.next_temp_slot >= MAX_FRAME_SLOTS {
167:         parser_error(proto_state, current_token(), "function uses too many values")
168:         return 0
169:     }
170: 
171:     slot := proto_state.next_temp_slot
172:     proto_state.next_temp_slot += 1
173:     return slot
174: }
175: 
176: // begin_scope starts one lexical block scope in the current proto.
177: // It records the local-count mark used when this scope exits.
178: begin_scope :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState) {
179: 	if proto_state.scope_depth >= MAX_FRAME_SLOTS {
180: 		parser_error(proto_state, current_token(), "too many nested scopes")
181: 		return
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	proto_state.scope_local_counts[proto_state.scope_depth] = proto_state.local_count
185: 	proto_state.scope_depth += 1
186: }
187: 
188: // end_scope exits one lexical block scope in the current proto.
189: // Locals declared in this scope are discarded by restoring the saved local-count mark.
190: end_scope :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState) {
191:     proto_state.scope_depth -= 1
192:     proto_state.local_count = proto_state.scope_local_counts[proto_state.scope_depth]
193:     proto_state.next_temp_slot = proto_state.local_count
194: }
195: 
196: // declare_local binds one identifier to the next local frame slot.
197: // Duplicate names are rejected within the current lexical scope only.
198: declare_local :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState, ident_token: Token) -> int {
199:     ident_name := ident_token.value.(string)
200: 
201:     scope_start := 0
202:     if proto_state.scope_depth > 0 {
203:         scope_start = proto_state.scope_local_counts[proto_state.scope_depth - 1]
204:     }
205: 
206:     for local_index := scope_start; local_index < proto_state.local_count; local_index += 1 {
207:         if proto_state.local_bindings[local_index].name == ident_name {
208:             parser_error(
209:                 proto_state,
210:                 ident_token,
211:                 fmt.tprintf("local variable `%s` is already declared in this scope", ident_name),
212:             )
213:             return 0
214:         }
215:     }
216: 
217:     if proto_state.local_count >= MAX_FRAME_SLOTS {
218:         parser_error(proto_state, ident_token, "too many local variables in function")
219:         return 0
220:     }
221: 
222:     slot := proto_state.local_count
223:     proto_state.local_bindings[proto_state.local_count] = LocalBinding{name = ident_name, frame_slot = slot}
224:     proto_state.local_count += 1
225:     proto_state.next_temp_slot = proto_state.local_count
226:     return slot
227: }
228: 
229: // resolve_local finds the nearest local binding by identifier name.
230: // Reverse scan returns the most recently declared matching local.
231: resolve_local :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState, ident_name: string) -> (slot: int, found: bool) {
232:     for local_index := proto_state.local_count - 1; local_index >= 0; local_index -= 1 {
233:         if proto_state.local_bindings[local_index].name == ident_name {
234:             return proto_state.local_bindings[local_index].frame_slot, true
235:         }
236:     }
237: 
238:     return 0, false
239: }
240: 
241: 
242: // Function literals ==============================================================================
243: 
244: // Function bodies consume braces but do not create an extra root scope.
245: // Parameters and top-level body locals live together in the function's root scope.
246: // Blocks inside the body still create scopes through normal statement parsing.
247: parse_function_body :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState) {
248: 	consume_token(proto_state, .LEFT_BRACE, "expected '{' to start function body")
249:     if Parser.failed {
250:         return
251:     }
252: 
253:     for !Parser.failed && !at_token(.RIGHT_BRACE) && !at_token(.EOF) {
254:         parse_statement(proto_state)
255:         if Parser.failed {
256:             return
257:         }
258:         proto_state.next_temp_slot = proto_state.local_count
259:     }
260: 
261:     if at_token(.EOF) {
262:         parser_error(proto_state, current_token(), "expected '}' to close function body")
263:         return
264:     }
265: 
266:     consume_token(proto_state, .RIGHT_BRACE, "expected '}' to close function body")
267: }
268: 
269: // parse_function_literal parses function(params...) { body } as a value expression.
270: // The compiled child proto is stored on the parent proto and loaded by LOAD_FUNC.
271: parse_function_literal :: proc(parent_proto_state: ^ProtoState, dst: int, function_name: string, origin_token: Token) {
272: 	consume_token(parent_proto_state, .FUNCTION, "expected 'function'")
273:     if Parser.failed {
274:         return
275:     }
276: 
277: 	consume_token(parent_proto_state, .LEFT_PAREN, "expected '(' after function")
278: 	if Parser.failed {
279: 		return
280: 	}
281: 
282: 	// Parse parameters first while still compiling the parent.
283: 	// The child proto is not created until the function signature is known.
284: 	param_tokens: [MAX_FRAME_SLOTS]Token
285: 	param_count := 0
286: 	if !at_token(.RIGHT_PAREN) {
287:         for {
288:             if param_count >= MAX_FRAME_SLOTS {
289:                 parser_error(parent_proto_state, current_token(), "too many function parameters")
290:                 return
291:             }
292: 
293:             param_tokens[param_count] = consume_token(parent_proto_state, .IDENT, "expected parameter name")
294:             if Parser.failed {
295:                 return
296:             }
297:             param_count += 1
298: 
299:             if !at_token(.COMMA) {
300:                 break
301:             }
302: 
303:             advance_token()
304:             if at_token(.RIGHT_PAREN) {
305:                 break
306:             }
307:         }
308:     }
309: 
310:     consume_token(parent_proto_state, .RIGHT_PAREN, "expected ')' after function parameters")
311: 	if Parser.failed {
312: 		return
313: 	}
314: 
315: 	// Build a fresh proto state for the function body.
316: 	// Parent proto state stays alive so the finished child can be appended to it.
317: 	child_origin := SourceLocation{
318: 		source_name = parent_proto_state.origin.source_name,
319: 		line        = origin_token.line,
320: 		column      = origin_token.column,
321: 	}
322: 	child_proto_state := begin_proto(child_origin, function_name, param_count)
323: 
324: 	// Parameters are local slots starting at slot 0.
325: 	// The VM call path places arguments directly into those slots.
326: 	for param_index := 0; param_index < param_count; param_index += 1 {
327: 		declare_local(&child_proto_state, param_tokens[param_index])
328: 		if Parser.failed {
329: 			delete_proto_state(&child_proto_state)
330:             return
331:         }
332:     }
333: 
334:     parse_function_body(&child_proto_state)
335:     if Parser.failed {
336: 		delete_proto_state(&child_proto_state)
337: 		return
338: 	}
339: 
340: 	// A function that reaches the closing brace returns nil.
341: 	return_slot := claim_temp_slot(&child_proto_state)
342: 	if Parser.failed {
343: 		delete_proto_state(&child_proto_state)
344: 		return
345:     }
346: 	emit_load_nil(&child_proto_state, return_slot)
347: 	emit_return(&child_proto_state, return_slot, 1)
348: 
349: 	// Store the compiled child proto on the parent, then emit LOAD_FUNC.
350: 	// LOAD_FUNC creates the runtime function object when the parent executes.
351:     if len(parent_proto_state.child_protos) >= MAX_CHILD_PROTOS {
352:         delete_proto_state(&child_proto_state)
353:         parser_error(parent_proto_state, origin_token, "too many functions in function")
354:         return
355:     }
356: 
357: 	child_proto := end_proto(&child_proto_state)
358: 	child_proto_index := len(parent_proto_state.child_protos)
359: 	append(&parent_proto_state.child_protos, child_proto)
360:     emit_load_func(parent_proto_state, dst, child_proto_index)
361: }
362: 
363: 
364: // Expressions ====================================================================================
365: 
366: // parse_primary emits one primary expression value into dst.
367: // IDENT resolves local first, then global binding table by name.
368: parse_primary :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState, dst: int) {
369: 	if at_token(.FUNCTION) {
370: 		parse_function_literal(proto_state, dst, "<function>", current_token())
371: 		return
372: 	}
373: 
374:     token := advance_token()
375: 
376:     #partial switch token.kind {
377:     case .INT:
378:         if len(proto_state.const_pool) >= MAX_CONST_POOL_ENTRIES {
379:             parser_error(proto_state, token, "too many constants in function")
380:             return
381:         }
382: 
383:         value := token.value.(i64)
384:         const_index := const_int(proto_state, value)
385:         emit_load_const(proto_state, dst, const_index)
386: 
387:     case .FLOAT:
388:         if len(proto_state.const_pool) >= MAX_CONST_POOL_ENTRIES {
389:             parser_error(proto_state, token, "too many constants in function")
390:             return
391:         }
392: 
393:         value := token.value.(f64)
394:         const_index := const_float(proto_state, value)
395:         emit_load_const(proto_state, dst, const_index)
396: 
397:     case .STRING:
398:         if len(proto_state.const_pool) >= MAX_CONST_POOL_ENTRIES {
399:             parser_error(proto_state, token, "too many constants in function")
400:             return
401:         }
402: 
403:         text := token.value.(string)
404:         const_index := const_string(proto_state, text)
405:         emit_load_const(proto_state, dst, const_index)
406: 
407:     case .TRUE:
408:         emit_load_true(proto_state, dst)
409: 
410:     case .FALSE:
411:         emit_load_false(proto_state, dst)
412: 
413:     case .NIL:
414:         emit_load_nil(proto_state, dst)
415: 
416:     case .IDENT:
417:         ident_name := token.value.(string)
418:         local_slot, is_local := resolve_local(proto_state, ident_name)
419:         if is_local {
420:             emit_move(proto_state, dst, local_slot)
421:         } else {
422:             binding_id, found_global := resolve_global(ident_name)
423:             if !found_global {
424:                 parser_error(proto_state, token, fmt.tprintf("unknown name `%s`", ident_name))
425:                 return
426:             }
427:             emit_get_global(proto_state, dst, binding_id)
428:         }
429: 
430: 	case:
431: 		parser_error(proto_state, token, fmt.tprintf("expected expression, got `%s`", token_text_for_error(token)))
432: 	}
433: }
434: 
435: // parse_unary parses prefix unary operators and primary expressions.
436: // Prefix NOT lowers by evaluating the operand first, then emitting NOT into dst.
437: parse_unary :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState, dst: int) {
438:     if at_token(.NOT) {
439:         advance_token()
440: 
441:         operand_slot := claim_temp_slot(proto_state)
442:         if Parser.failed {
443:             return
444:         }
445: 
446:         parse_unary(proto_state, operand_slot)
447:         if Parser.failed {
448:             return
449:         }
450: 
451:         emit_not(proto_state, dst, operand_slot)
452:         return
453:     }
454: 
455:     parse_primary(proto_state, dst)
456:     if Parser.failed {
457:         return
458:     }
459: 
460:     if at_token(.LEFT_PAREN) {
461:         parse_call(proto_state, dst, 1)
462:     }
463: }
464: 
465: // parse_call lowers callee(args...) using contiguous call slots:
466: // callee_slot, arg0, arg1, ...
467: // requested_results controls VM CALL result shaping (0 for statements, 1 for expressions).
468: parse_call :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState, callee_slot, requested_results: int) {
469:     consume_token(proto_state, .LEFT_PAREN, "expected '(' to start call arguments")
470:     if Parser.failed {
471:         return
472:     }
473: 
474:     arg_count := 0
475:     if !at_token(.RIGHT_PAREN) {
476:         for {
477:             arg_slot := callee_slot + 1 + arg_count
478:             if arg_slot >= MAX_FRAME_SLOTS {
479:                 parser_error(proto_state, current_token(), "call uses too many values")
480:                 return
481:             }
482: 
483:             parse_expression(proto_state, arg_slot)
484:             if Parser.failed {
485:                 return
486:             }
487:             arg_count += 1
488: 
489:             if !at_token(.COMMA) {
490:                 break
491:             }
492: 
493:             advance_token()
494:             if at_token(.RIGHT_PAREN) {
495:                 break
496:             }
497:         }
498:     }
499: 
500:     consume_token(proto_state, .RIGHT_PAREN, "expected ')' after call arguments")
501:     if Parser.failed {
502:         return
503:     }
504:     emit_call(proto_state, callee_slot, arg_count, requested_results)
505: }
506: 
507: // parse_expression currently supports unary expressions and call suffix on primaries.
508: // Operator precedence parsing is not in this stage yet.
509: parse_expression :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState, dst: int) {
510:     parse_unary(proto_state, dst)
511: }
512: 
513: 
514: // Statements =====================================================================================
515: 
516: // parse_block parses one braced statement block.
517: // Blocks create a new lexical local scope.
518: parse_block :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState) {
519:     consume_token(proto_state, .LEFT_BRACE, "expected '{' to start block")
520:     if Parser.failed {
521:         return
522: 	}
523: 
524: 	begin_scope(proto_state)
525: 	if Parser.failed {
526: 		return
527: 	}
528: 
529: 	for !Parser.failed && !at_token(.RIGHT_BRACE) && !at_token(.EOF) {
530:         parse_statement(proto_state)
531:         if Parser.failed {
532:             return
533:         }
534:         proto_state.next_temp_slot = proto_state.local_count
535:     }
536: 
537:     if at_token(.EOF) {
538:         parser_error(proto_state, current_token(), "expected '}' to close block")
539:         return
540:     }
541: 
542:     consume_token(proto_state, .RIGHT_BRACE, "expected '}' to close block")
543:     if Parser.failed {
544:         return
545:     }
546: 
547:     end_scope(proto_state)
548: }
549: 
550: // parse_if_statement parses:
551: // if <expression> { <statements> } [else { <statements> }]
552: parse_if_statement :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState) {
553:     consume_token(proto_state, .IF, "expected 'if'")
554:     if Parser.failed {
555:         return
556:     }
557: 
558:     condition_slot := claim_temp_slot(proto_state)
559:     if Parser.failed {
560:         return
561:     }
562: 
563:     parse_expression(proto_state, condition_slot)
564:     if Parser.failed {
565:         return
566:     }
567: 
568:     false_jump := emit_jump_false(proto_state, condition_slot)
569:     parse_block(proto_state)
570:     if Parser.failed {
571:         return
572:     }
573: 
574:     if at_token(.ELSE) {
575:         advance_token()
576: 
577:         end_jump := emit_jump(proto_state)
578:         patch_jump(proto_state, false_jump)
579:         if Parser.failed {
580:             return
581:         }
582: 
583:         if at_token(.IF) {
584:             parse_if_statement(proto_state)
585:         } else {
586:             parse_block(proto_state)
587:         }
588:         if Parser.failed {
589:             return
590:         }
591: 
592:         patch_jump(proto_state, end_jump)
593:         if Parser.failed {
594:             return
595:         }
596:         return
597:     }
598: 
599:     patch_jump(proto_state, false_jump)
600:     if Parser.failed {
601:         return
602:     }
603: }
604: 
605: // parse_for_statement parses:
606: // for <expression> { <statements> }
607: // for { <statements> }
608: // Condition form evaluates each iteration.
609: // Braced form is infinite-loop sugar with no condition-exit jump.
610: parse_for_statement :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState) {
611:     consume_token(proto_state, .FOR, "expected 'for'")
612:     if Parser.failed {
613:         return
614:     }
615: 
616:     if proto_state.loop_depth >= MAX_LOOP_DEPTH {
617:         parser_error(proto_state, current_token(), "too many nested loops")
618:         return
619:     }
620:     proto_state.loop_break_fixup_base[proto_state.loop_depth] = proto_state.break_fixup_count
621:     proto_state.loop_depth += 1
622: 
623:     loop_start := next_inst_index(proto_state)
624:     has_exit_jump := false
625:     exit_jump := 0
626: 
627:     // `for { ... }` has no condition expression.
628:     // It loops until `break`, `return`, or runtime termination.
629:     if !at_token(.LEFT_BRACE) {
630:         condition_slot := claim_temp_slot(proto_state)
631:         if Parser.failed {
632:             return
633:         }
634: 
635:         parse_expression(proto_state, condition_slot)
636:         if Parser.failed {
637:             return
638:         }
639: 
640:         exit_jump = emit_jump_false(proto_state, condition_slot)
641:         has_exit_jump = true
642:     }
643: 
644:     parse_block(proto_state)
645:     if Parser.failed {
646:         return
647:     }
648: 
649:     emit_jump(proto_state, loop_start)
650:     if Parser.failed {
651:         return
652:     }
653: 
654:     if has_exit_jump {
655:         patch_jump(proto_state, exit_jump)
656:         if Parser.failed {
657:             return
658:         }
659:     }
660: 
661:     proto_state.loop_depth -= 1
662:     break_fixup_base := proto_state.loop_break_fixup_base[proto_state.loop_depth]
663:     for fixup_index := break_fixup_base; fixup_index < proto_state.break_fixup_count; fixup_index += 1 {
664:         patch_jump(proto_state, proto_state.break_fixups[fixup_index])
665:         if Parser.failed {
666:             return
667:         }
668:     }
669:     proto_state.break_fixup_count = break_fixup_base
670: }
671: 
672: // parse_break_statement parses:
673: // break
674: // break is only valid inside loop bodies.
675: parse_break_statement :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState) {
676:     break_token := consume_token(proto_state, .BREAK, "expected 'break'")
677:     if Parser.failed {
678:         return
679:     }
680: 
681:     if proto_state.loop_depth == 0 {
682:         parser_error(proto_state, break_token, "break is only valid inside loops")
683:         return
684:     }
685: 
686:     if proto_state.break_fixup_count >= MAX_BREAK_FIXUPS {
687:         parser_error(proto_state, break_token, "function has too many break statements")
688:         return
689:     }
690: 
691:     break_jump := emit_jump(proto_state)
692:     proto_state.break_fixups[proto_state.break_fixup_count] = break_jump
693:     proto_state.break_fixup_count += 1
694: }
695: 
696: // parse_return_statement parses:
697: // - return
698: // - return expr
699: // - return expr, expr, ...
700: // Each return expression is lowered as a single-result expression.
701: parse_return_statement :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState) {
702:     consume_token(proto_state, .RETURN, "expected 'return'")
703:     if Parser.failed {
704:         return
705:     }
706: 
707:     if at_token(.EOF) || at_token(.RIGHT_BRACE) {
708:         emit_return(proto_state, 0, 0)
709:         return
710:     }
711: 
712:     first_result_slot := claim_temp_slot(proto_state)
713:     if Parser.failed {
714:         return
715:     }
716: 
717:     parse_expression(proto_state, first_result_slot)
718:     if Parser.failed {
719:         return
720:     }
721: 
722:     result_count := 1
723:     for at_token(.COMMA) {
724:         advance_token()
725: 
726:         result_slot := first_result_slot + result_count
727:         if result_slot >= MAX_FRAME_SLOTS {
728:             parser_error(proto_state, current_token(), "return has too many values")
729:             return
730:         }
731: 
732:         parse_expression(proto_state, result_slot)
733:         if Parser.failed {
734:             return
735:         }
736:         result_count += 1
737:     }
738: 
739:     emit_return(proto_state, first_result_slot, result_count)
740: }
741: 
742: // parse_statement supports:
743: // - if expression block [else block]
744: // - for expression block
745: // - break
746: // - return [expr [, expr ...]]
747: // - ident := expression
748: // - ident = expression (local-only assignment)
749: // - call statement
750: parse_statement :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState) {
751:     if at_token(.RETURN) {
752:         parse_return_statement(proto_state)
753:         return
754:     }
755: 
756:     if at_token(.IF) {
757:         parse_if_statement(proto_state)
758:         return
759:     }
760: 
761:     if at_token(.FOR) {
762:         parse_for_statement(proto_state)
763:         return
764:     }
765: 
766:     if at_token(.BREAK) {
767:         parse_break_statement(proto_state)
768:         return
769:     }
770: 
771: 	if !at_token(.IDENT) {
772: 		token := current_token()
773: 		parser_error(proto_state, token, fmt.tprintf("expected statement, got `%s`", token_text_for_error(token)))
774: 		return
775: 	}
776: 
777:     next_kind := peek_token().kind
778:     if next_kind == .LEFT_PAREN {
779:         callee_slot := claim_temp_slot(proto_state)
780:         if Parser.failed {
781:             return
782:         }
783: 
784:         parse_primary(proto_state, callee_slot)
785:         if Parser.failed {
786:             return
787:         }
788: 
789:         parse_call(proto_state, callee_slot, 0)
790:         return
791:     }
792: 
793:     ident_token := advance_token()
794:     ident_text := ident_token.value.(string)
795: 
796:     if next_kind == .DECL {
797:         advance_token()
798: 
799:         slot := declare_local(proto_state, ident_token)
800:         if Parser.failed {
801: 			return
802: 		}
803: 
804: 		// Declaration gives the function proto a useful name and origin.
805: 		if at_token(.FUNCTION) {
806: 			parse_function_literal(proto_state, slot, ident_text, ident_token)
807: 			return
808: 		}
809: 
810:         parse_expression(proto_state, slot)
811:         return
812:     }
813: 
814:     if next_kind == .ASSIGN {
815:         slot, is_local := resolve_local(proto_state, ident_text)
816:         if !is_local {
817:             parser_error(proto_state, ident_token, fmt.tprintf("assignment target `%s` is not a local variable", ident_text))
818:             return
819:         }
820: 
821:         advance_token()
822:         parse_expression(proto_state, slot)
823:         return
824:     }
825: 
826:     parser_error(
827:         proto_state,
828:         ident_token,
829:         fmt.tprintf("bare expression `%s` is not a statement; expected declaration, assignment, or call", ident_text),
830:     )
831: }
832: 
833: // parse_top_level_statements parses until EOF or first parse failure.
834: // After each statement, next_temp_slot resets to local_count so temporary slots are reused.
835: parse_top_level_statements :: proc(proto_state: ^ProtoState) {
836:     for !Parser.failed && !at_token(.EOF) {
837:         parse_statement(proto_state)
838:         if Parser.failed {
839:             return
840:         }
841:         proto_state.next_temp_slot = proto_state.local_count
842:     }
843: }
844: 
845: 
846: // Source compilation =============================================================================
847: 
848: // compile_source scans source, parses top-level forms, and builds entry proto.
849: // On success it installs Active_State.entry_function for VM execution.
850: compile_source :: proc(source, source_name: string) -> ^Error {
851:     tokens, scan_error := scan_source(source, source_name)
852:     defer delete(tokens)
853:     if scan_error != nil {
854:         return scan_error
855:     }
856: 
857:     Parser.tokens = tokens[:]
858:     Parser.token_index = 0
859:     Parser.failed = false
860: 
861: 	entry_origin := SourceLocation{
862: 		source_name = source_name,
863: 		line        = 1,
864: 		column      = 1,
865: 	}
866: 	entry_proto_state := begin_proto(entry_origin, "entry", 0)
867:     parse_top_level_statements(&entry_proto_state)
868:     if Parser.failed {
869: 		delete_proto_state(&entry_proto_state)
870:         return &Active_State.error
871:     }
872: 
873:     // Source fallthrough is defined as implicit `return nil`.
874:     // This keeps entry completion on the same RETURN path as explicit source returns.
875:     return_slot := claim_temp_slot(&entry_proto_state)
876:     if Parser.failed {
877: 		delete_proto_state(&entry_proto_state)
878:         return &Active_State.error
879:     }
880:     emit_load_nil(&entry_proto_state, return_slot)
881:     emit_return(&entry_proto_state, return_slot, 1)
882: 
883:     entry_proto := end_proto(&entry_proto_state)
884:     entry_function := new(ProtoFunctionObject)
885:     entry_function^ = ProtoFunctionObject{
886:         header = Object{kind = .PROTO_FUNCTION},
887:         name   = entry_proto.name,
888:         impl   = entry_proto,
889:     }
890:     Active_State.entry_function = entry_function
891:     return nil
892: }

(End of file - total 892 lines)
</content>
